(1) tradeRoute can only be road/river/port/crossroads/isolated/mountain_pass — from resolveConfig.js TERRAIN_ROUTE_POOLS (lines 20-28) and route resolution (101-119), and the ConfigurationPanel.jsx select (363-370); never 'city'/'metropolis'. (2) economicGenerator.js:1488 tests route === 'city'||'metropolis' and 1491 tests route === 'metropolis' — both unreachable; 1491 is doubly dead because the fromHinterland else-if at 1490 always matches first for city tier. (3) isFromHigher is hardwired false at the only live call site (1221, reached via generateEconomicState:1916 ← steps/generateEconomy.js:133); the other call site (973) is inside _computeIncomeStreams (956) which has no callers — so village fromHigher is unreachable too. (4) The fromHigher/fromCityOrMetropolis/fromMetropolis buckets in UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER are dead data, duplicated dead in data/tradeGoodsData.js IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER and servicesGenerator.js _UPGRADE_CHAINS. (5) Blast radius confirmed: q (primaryImports) is unconditionally overwritten with chainImports at 2327-2328, so the only live output of getUpgradeChain is the entrepôt transit list (1239 → I at 2134 → transit: I at 2535), consumed by deriveRegionalState.js:74 (regional trade flows), EventComposer.jsx:152, generateEconomy.js:63/75, and events/mutate.js — entrepôt towns/cities list hinterland goods (Food surplus, Raw wool and hides, Timber / Bulk food, Raw materials) as through-trade instead of the intended luxury/banking goods. No comments or tests indicate the dead branches are intentional. Severity medium is right: primary dossier imports/exports are chain-derived and unaffected; the wrong output is confined to the transit list and the three dead data tables, a real but narrow simulation-coherence defect.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested direction is right, with three additions: (a) any fix must also reorder/restructure the branch chain — line 1491 (fromMetropolis) is dead independently of the route comparison because the unconditional `else if (tierData.fromHinterland)` at 1490 precedes it and city tier always has fromHinterland; (b) a settlement realistically imports BOTH hinterland staples and higher-tier goods, so consider merging buckets (e.g. fromHinterland + fromCityOrMetropolis for connected towns) rather than picking one, which also keeps existing fromHinterland output stable; (c) clean up the seam properly: _computeIncomeStreams (line 956) is an uncalled duplicate of generateTradeIncomeStreams and should be deleted, and the mirrored dead buckets in data/tradeGoodsData.js IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER and servicesGenerator.js _UPGRADE_CHAINS should be wired or removed in the same change. Note changing the transit list will perturb snapshot tests — expected, but plan for it.

verdicts.reasoning: Fully reproduced. (1) Route value space confirmed: resolveConfig.js:20-27/109-119 and ConfigurationPanel.jsx:363-369 only ever produce isolated/road/river/port/crossroads — 'city'/'metropolis' are settlement-type values, never routes. (2) Call chain confirmed: generateEconomy.js:133 → generateEconomicState (economicGenerator.js:1674) → generateTradeIncomeStreams (1916) → getUpgradeChain(tier, route, false, …) (1221). Only other call site (973) is inside _computeIncomeStreams, which has zero callers. So the route==='city'||'metropolis' tests (1488) and route==='metropolis' (1491) are unreachable, and isFromHigher is always false — village fromHigher (1339-1358), town fromCityOrMetropolis (1361-1392), and city fromMetropolis (1415-1434) are dead data. Bonus: line 1491 is doubly dead because the fromHinterland else-if (1490) precedes it and city.fromHinterland exists. (3) Blast radius confirmed: q (primaryImports) is unconditionally overwritten with chainImports at 2327-2328; the pre-override re built from v.transit (2123-2127) is likewise wiped at 2322; the surviving consumer is transit (1239 → I at 2134 → returned at 2535). (4) Demonstrated with a read-only node run of the real module: town@crossroads entrepôt yields transit ["Food surplus","Raw wool and hides","Timber"]; city@crossroads yields ["Bulk food","Raw materials","Basic goods for resale"] — hinterland inflows mislabeled as entrepôt through-trade instead of the intended luxury/banking lists. (5) transit consumers: deriveRegionalState.js:69-75 counts transit among the settlement's EXPORT labels for regional supply/demand matching (entrepôt towns advertise hinterland staples as re-exports to the region), EventComposer.jsx:152 trade-good union, domain/events/mutate.js transit bucket. Neither OutputContainer.jsx nor pdf/lib/viewModel.js renders transit, so the main dossier/PDF trade panels are unaffected — wrong-but-plausible simulation output on a common config (crossroads/port towns and cities), not a crash or main-path corruption. Severity medium: real coherence defect in a live field plus three curated import tables that are dead data, but primary imports/exports are chain-derived by design and unaffected.
verdicts.fix_note: A naive fix (just making the comparisons match real route values) is not possible — no route value means 'connected to a city/metropolis'. Corrections for any fix: (a) Branch order matters: city.fromHinterland exists, so the fromMetropolis check at 1491 must move ABOVE the fromHinterland fallback at 1490 or it stays unreachable even with a correct signal. (b) The cleanest live fix is scoped to transit semantics: entrepôt through-trade (line 1239) should draw from the higher-tier buckets (town.fromCityOrMetropolis / city.fromMetropolis) since transit is by definition through-trade, while the imports fallback to fromHinterland is arguably correct for the imports list itself; alternatively derive a 'connected to higher market' signal (e.g., crossroads/port route, or neighbour profile tier) and pass it explicitly instead of overloading route. (c) The isService filter inside getUpgradeChain drops 2 of 3 village fromHigher entries anyway; wiring isFromHigher=true needs a real upstream signal or the bucket should be removed. (d) Three near-identical copies of these tables exist and must stay in sync or be unified: economicGenerator.js UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER (1267), servicesGenerator.js _UPGRADE_CHAINS (425), tradeGoodsData.js IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER (347 — note customRegistry.js:253 ingests this one with bucket keys like 'fromHinterland' treated as good names, a separate adjacent seam). (e) Changing getUpgradeChain output changes generated settlements — seed-pinned snapshot/property tests will need regeneration. (f) Dead _computeIncomeStreams (956-996) should be deleted as part of the cleanup; the `${tier}_import_${name}` goodsToggles keys (1493) are generated by no UI and are dormant too.

confirmed.title: Legacy heuristic export/import writers (military services, entire slave-trade economy block, toggle-forced local production) are computed then unconditionally discarded by chain derivation
confirmed.evidence: Lines 2113-2213 build the heuristic export list `re` and import list `q`: siege/occupation suffixes (2117-2127), goods-toggle force/exclude into `re` and `v.localProduction` (2135-2158), military-services exports (2175-2185), and the slave-trade block (2186-2213) which rolls `_rng() < ge` (line 2195) and pushes slave-trade export/import labels. At lines 2322-2328 `re.length = 0` / `q.length = 0` and both are refilled exclusively from chainExports/serviceExports/chainImports; `v.localProduction` is likewise cleared and refilled from chainLocalProd at 2345-2348. Siege/occupation suffixes and `_good_` toggles ARE replicated inside deriveExportsFromChains (computeActiveChains.js:600-623), but: (a) the slave-trade roll consumes an rng draw whose entire output is discarded — no materialized chain-side equivalent produces those labels; (b) the military-services heuristic (garrison/barracks/mercenary keywords, militaryEffective>=60) is only partially replaced by INSTITUTIONAL_SERVICE_MAP's much narrower patterns ('mercenary quarter','multiple garrison','professional city watch'); (c) the toggle force-add into localProduction (2147-2151) has no counterpart — deriveLocalProductionFromChains has no toggle handling — so forcing a good never surfaces it in localProduction.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Either delete the dead writers (and accept the rng-stream shift behind a version flag) or move the slave-trade/military heuristics after the chain override so their outputs land in the final lists; add goodsToggles handling to deriveLocalProductionFromChains.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified at the code level. (1) The override is real: economicGenerator.js:2322-2328 wipes the heuristic export list `re` and import list `q` and refills them exclusively from chain-derived values; v.localProduction is likewise wiped/refilled at 2345-2348. Nothing reads re/q between the heuristic writers (2113-2213) and the wipe, and the function is on the live pipeline path (steps/generateEconomy.js:133). The comment at computeActiveChains.js:420-422 explicitly says chain derivation "replaces" the heuristic computation — so the entire legacy block (siege/occupation suffixes, toggle handling, military services, slave-trade roll) is executed-then-discarded dead computation on every generation, including a seeded rng draw at line 2195 (config-deterministic, so not a determinism break, but pure waste that pins the stream layout to dead code). (2) The strongest functional claim, (c), is fully confirmed: deriveLocalProductionFromChains (computeActiveChains.js:717-732) has no goodsToggles handling and no code after the refill applies toggles to localProduction, while deriveExportsFromChains DOES apply force/exclude toggles (lines 611-623). Result: a DM-banned good vanishes from exports but can still appear in the user-visible localProduction list (EconomicsTab.jsx:338, PDF EconomicsTrade.jsx:111), and a forced good shows in exports but never in localProduction — a real incoherence lost in the strangler-fig migration, with zero test coverage of goodsToggles to pin it as intended. (3) Claims (a) and (b) are partially overstated: the slave economy is NOT entirely absent chain-side — supplyChainData.js:1448-1464 defines an exportable slave_trade chain gated on 'Slave market'/'Workhouse' institutions — and the military-services narrowing carries an explicit design comment ("requires substantial military infrastructure (city+)", computeActiveChains.js:755), suggesting intentional redesigns rather than accidental loss. Net: real unfinished seam with one genuine behavioral regression (toggles→localProduction) plus substantial dead code including a wasted rng draw. Medium severity is honest: edge-case, DM-toggle-dependent, user-visible incoherence, not a main-path break.
verdicts.fix_note: Two corrections to the suggested fix. (1) Do NOT move the slave-trade heuristic after the chain override: an institution-gated slave_trade chain already exists (supplyChainData.js:1448-1464, exportable, gated on 'Slave market'/'Workhouse'), so resurrecting the probabilistic roll would double-represent the slave economy and contradict the chain-first, institution-coherent design. Delete the dead block instead; note the deletion shifts the seeded rng stream for tier>=town settlements (the draw at 2195 is conditional on tier), so snapshot/property tests will churn — the suggested version flag is appropriate. (2) The military-services narrowing appears intentional (comment at computeActiveChains.js:755: "requires substantial military infrastructure (city+)"); just delete the dead heuristic rather than porting it, unless a design decision says otherwise. The fix that actually matters is the third one: add goodsToggles force/exclude handling to deriveLocalProductionFromChains (or apply the toggles after the refill at economicGenerator.js:2345-2348). The allow===false exclusion is the sharper half — a banned good currently disappears from exports but persists in localProduction, an internal dossier/PDF incoherence. Also remove the now-dead toggle writes into v.localProduction at 2147-2156 and add a test pinning toggle behavior across exports AND localProduction, since no test currently exercises goodsToggles at all.

verdicts.reasoning: The mechanical claim is accurate: economicGenerator.js heuristic writers (lines 2113-2213, toggle pass 2135-2158) are computed then unconditionally discarded by the chain-derivation override (re/q cleared at 2322-2328, v.localProduction at 2345-2348), and those arrays are the returned primaryExports/primaryImports/localProduction rendered in dossier (EconomicsTab.jsx:278-341) and PDF (viewModel.js:542). However, only one of the three sub-claims is a real defect. (a) Slave trade: FALSE — a materialized chain-side equivalent exists: the 'slave_trade' chain in supplyChainData.js:1448-1464 (exportable:true, outputs 'Forced labour services'/'Domestic servants'), institution-gated on 'Slave market'/'Workhouse' which exist in institutionalCatalog.js (975, 1568, 1575, 2095); runtime repro confirmed a city with 'Slave market district' exports those labels. The dead rng draw at 2195 is deterministic and harmless dead code, not lost behavior — the migration deliberately replaced a probabilistic roll with institution-gated derivation. (b) Military services: the narrowing carries an explicit design comment 'requires substantial military infrastructure (city+)' (computeActiveChains.js:755), so it reads intentional; the only genuine residual gap is 'Free company hall' (institutionalCatalog.js:1291, a mercenary billet explicitly selling military hire) matching neither INSTITUTIONAL_SERVICE_MAP nor an exportable chain — low severity. (c) Toggle/localProduction: TRUE and reproduced end-to-end with the real modules. Forcing 'town_good_Cedar timber' puts it in primaryExports AND spawns income source 'Cedar timber Trade' described as 'Revenue from locally produced cedar timber', yet localProduction omits it; excluding 'town_good_Grain' removes it from exports but localProduction still lists 'grain'. deriveLocalProductionFromChains (computeActiveChains.js:717-732) has no toggle handling and no downstream pass applies toggles. This is a real, user-visible broken seam of the strangler-fig migration: a DM-facing feature (TradeDynamicsPanel force/exclude) half-works and produces an internally contradictory dossier and PDF. Net: real defect, but materially narrower than claimed — medium (edge-case requiring toggles, real coherence/seam break), not a wholesale loss of three feature blocks.
verdicts.fix_note: Corrections to the implied fix: (1) Do NOT port the slave-trade block into chain derivation — the chain-side equivalent already exists (supplyChainData.js 'slave_trade' chain); simply delete the dead block at economicGenerator.js:2186-2213. Note that removing the dead _rng() draw shifts the seeded rng stream, changing downstream output for existing seeds — snapshot/property tests pinning seeded settlements will need regeneration. (2) The real fix is for localProduction toggles: either add a goodsToggles parameter to deriveLocalProductionFromChains, or (simpler) move the existing force/exclude loop (2135-2158) to run AFTER the chainLocalProd refill at 2345-2348, applying it only to v.localProduction (exports already get toggle handling inside deriveExportsFromChains:611-623 — do not double-apply there). Run it before the subsumeTradeGoods pass at 2404-2408 so labels normalize. (3) For military services, do not resurrect the broad militaryEffective>=60 heuristic — the city+ narrowing is documented design; instead add 'free company hall' to the INSTITUTIONAL_SERVICE_MAP military patterns (computeActiveChains.js:756) to cover the town-tier mercenary billet. (4) The siege/occupation suffix construction (2117-2127) and the q assembly (2128-2132) are also dead and deletable, but keep the U mutations at 2113-2116 — U is still live (passed to deriveImportsFromChains and returned as necessityImports).

confirmed.title: Goods-toggle key vocabulary split: generator reads tier_export_/tier_import_ booleans while the store's canonical format is tier_good_ objects — exclusion objects would read as 'on'
confirmed.evidence: store/toggleSlice.js documents exactly one Good key format: `tier_good_name` → `{ allow, force, forceExclude }` (line 8) and bulkSetGoods writes `${t}_good_${name}` objects (line 136). But getGoodsModifiers reads `o[`${r}_export_${m}`]` (economicGenerator.js:1252) and getUpgradeChain reads `goodsToggles[`${tier}_import_${item.name}`]` (line 1493), both treating the value as a boolean (`o[g] !== void 0 ? o[g] : h.on`). No materialized code writes `_export_`/`_import_` keys (grep over src finds only these readers). Two failure modes: per-good export/import toggles via these paths are dead keys; and if any caller ever passes the canonical object value, `{allow:false, force:false, forceExclude:true}` is truthy — an explicitly excluded good would be treated as toggled ON. The `_good_` object-shape handling that does work (deriveExportsFromChains lines 611-623, generateEconomicState 2079-2094) only operates on final lists, masking the inconsistency.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Unify on the canonical `tier_good_name` key + object shape: have getGoodsModifiers/getUpgradeChain read `goodsToggles[`${tier}_good_${name}`]` and respect .allow/.force/.forceExclude rather than truthiness.

verdicts.reasoning: Every factual claim verified by reading the code. (1) The canonical goods-toggle vocabulary is `tier_good_name` → {allow,force,forceExclude}: documented at src/store/toggleSlice.js:8, written by bulkSetGoods (toggleSlice.js:136) and by the only goods UI, TradeDynamicsPanel.jsx goodKey/cycleGood (lines 38, 135-146). (2) That exact store object is what reaches the generator: settlementSlice.js:535/858 → resolveConfig.js:72 → generateEconomy.js:133 → generateEconomicState → generateTradeIncomeStreams. (3) getGoodsModifiers (economicGenerator.js:1252), getHistoryModifiers (1048), and getUpgradeChain (1493) read `${tier}_export_${name}` / `${tier}_import_${name}` keys as booleans; grep over src/ and tests/ confirms no writer of those keys exists anywhere — the lookups always miss and fall back to the `on` defaults, so the goodsToggles parameter is inert in all three functions. This is a genuine unfinished seam. Severity calibration: the user-visible damage is narrower than the title suggests. primaryExports is wiped and rebuilt from deriveExportsFromChains (economicGenerator.js:2321-2326), which DOES apply the canonical _good_ toggles (computeActiveChains.js:611-623), so export force/exclude works in the dossier. getHistoryModifiers' only caller (_computeIncomeStreams, line 956/967) is itself never invoked (dead). The real behavioral gap is imports/transit: getUpgradeChain's output survives as `transit` (returned at line 2535, consumed by EventComposer.jsx:152), and primaryImports is rebuilt from deriveImportsFromChains (computeActiveChains.js:641) which takes no goodsToggles at all — so the "Import Goods" toggle section in TradeDynamicsPanel (line 188) silently has no effect on imports or transit. The hypothetical 'exclusion object reads as ON' failure cannot trigger today because _good_ keys never match the _export_/_import_ lookups. Net: real dead-key vocabulary split + a silently non-functional import-toggle feature = medium (broken seam / edge-case wrong output), not high, because the primary export-toggle path works through the chain-derived override.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix (unify on tier_good_name + object shape in getGoodsModifiers/getUpgradeChain) is directionally right but incomplete and partly cosmetic: (a) fixing getGoodsModifiers changes nothing observable — its exports are unconditionally discarded at economicGenerator.js:2321-2326 in favor of deriveExportsFromChains, which already applies toggles correctly; same for getHistoryModifiers, whose only caller _computeIncomeStreams (line 956) is dead code. (b) The fix with real user-visible effect is the imports side: deriveImportsFromChains (computeActiveChains.js:641) takes no goodsToggles parameter at all, so excluded goods still appear in primaryImports regardless of key format — it needs the same _good_ force/exclude pass deriveExportsFromChains has at lines 611-623. (c) getUpgradeChain should be fixed (its output survives as the returned `transit` list, consumed by EventComposer.jsx:152) using .forceExclude/.force/.allow semantics, not truthiness. (d) Same seam family: the _good_ post-processing block at economicGenerator.js:2135-2158 mutates re/localProduction that are subsequently wiped at 2321-2326/2345-2348 — also dead and worth removing in the same cleanup.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified end-to-end. (1) Vocabulary split confirmed: all writers produce `${tier}_good_${name}` object values (TradeDynamicsPanel.jsx:38/141/145-146, toggleSlice.js:136, tests wizardCloseout.test.js:60-61); the readers at economicGenerator.js:1252 (`_export_`), :1048 (`_export_`, in getHistoryModifiers), and :1493 (`_import_`) read keys with zero writers anywhere in src. (2) Demonstrated a concrete user-visible failure with node against the real modules: for a town/crossroads (entrepôt) settlement, excluding Timber via the canonical key (`town_good_Timber: {forceExclude:true}`) leaves "Timber" in economicState.transit unchanged, while the phantom legacy key `town_import_Timber: false` removes it — proving getUpgradeChain's reader is live for the transit output but only reachable via a vocabulary no UI can produce. economicState.transit feeds deriveRegionalState.js:74 (counted among regional EXPORT labels) and EventComposer.jsx:152 (trade-goods picker), so an explicitly excluded good leaks into the regional simulation and editor. (3) Scope caveat that tempers severity: most output of the dead readers is discarded by the chain-first override — `re` and `q` are unconditionally wiped at economicGenerator.js:2322/2327 and replaced by deriveExportsFromChains/deriveImportsFromChains output, and deriveExportsFromChains DOES apply `_good_` objects correctly (computeActiveChains.js:611-623; repro confirms dossier exports respect exclusions). getHistoryModifiers' caller `_computeIncomeStreams` (:956) has no callers at all. (4) The claimed "exclusion object read as ON" inversion is latent-only: the key vocabularies never collide, so `o[g]` is always undefined and falls through to defaults; it would only fire if the key name were fixed without fixing the value-shape handling. Net: real broken seam — per-good toggles dead through these readers, with a demonstrated leak of excluded goods into transit/regional state — but not wholesale wrong main-path output, hence medium.
verdicts.fix_note: Three corrections to the suggested fix. (a) A bare key rename to `_good_` WITHOUT value-shape normalization would CREATE the inversion bug the claim warns about ({allow:false,force:false,forceExclude:true} is truthy); any fix must normalize: undefined → def.on; object → forceExclude ? false : (force ? true : allow !== false); boolean → as-is. (b) Fixing getGoodsModifiers (1252) is pointless for output — its result only seeds `re`, which is unconditionally discarded at economicGenerator.js:2322 in favor of deriveExportsFromChains (which already handles `_good_` objects); also note getGoodsModifiers consumes `_rng()` per enabled good, so wiring toggles into it shifts downstream rng draws for toggle-bearing configs. Prefer deleting the dead gate (and the uncalled _computeIncomeStreams/getHistoryModifiers pair at :956/:1043, plus the dead-effect `_good_` block at :2135-2158 that mutates lists wiped at :2322/:2345). (c) The one reachable repair is getUpgradeChain (1493), whose output survives as economicState.transit (via generateTradeIncomeStreams :1239) into deriveRegionalState.js:74 and EventComposer.jsx:152 — either unify it on `_good_` keys with object normalization, or apply the same force/exclude pass deriveExportsFromChains uses to the transit list. Note even after that, per-good toggles still won't affect primaryImports (deriveImportsFromChains and demand imports never consult goodsToggles; finalizeTradeLists doesn't either) — a complete import-side fix needs a toggle pass on the final import list. Related same-family defect worth fixing together: institutionProbability.js:13 and structuralValidator.js:158 check `goodsToggles[key] === false`, which never matches the object values the UI writes (value-shape split rather than key split).

confirmed.title: GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE referenced but never defined — 'Enslaved persons' goods get category undefined; same records also use a 'requiresInstitution' key the readers don't recognize
confirmed.evidence: GOODS_CATEGORIES (lines 10-17) defines AGRICULTURAL/RAW_MATERIALS/MANUFACTURED/LUXURY/SERVICES/FOOD_PROCESSED — no TRADE. Yet `category: GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE` is used at lines 131 (EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town), 203 (city), and 390 (IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town), yielding `category: undefined`. The same town/city 'Enslaved persons' export records use `requiresInstitution` (lines 135, 207) while every other record and both readers (getGoodsModifiers/getHistoryModifiers in economicGenerator.js, lines 1254/1051) use `requiredInstitution` — so even where these records were read, the institution gate would never apply. Currently inert because EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER's only materialized consumer (EventComposer.jsx:262) uses keys only, but it is a live landmine for any consumer that groups by category or honors institution gates.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add TRADE: 'trade' to GOODS_CATEGORIES (or recategorize the records), and rename requiresInstitution → requiredInstitution on all three records.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified directly in src/data/tradeGoodsData.js: GOODS_CATEGORIES (lines 10-17) has no TRADE key, yet lines 131, 203, 390 use GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE, yielding category: undefined on all three 'Enslaved persons' records. Lines 135/207 use requiresInstitution while the file's own JSDoc (line 24) and every other record use requiredInstitution; project-wide grep shows nothing ever reads requiresInstitution. No guard exists (validate:data only finds duplicate keys). The claim slightly understates the live impact: besides EventComposer.jsx:262 (keys-only, confirmed), customRegistry.js:241 (enumeratePrebuiltTradeGoods) reads props?.category || 'other' from both EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER and IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER, and EntityPicker.jsx displays/searches that subcategory — so the undefined category already manifests today as a generic 'other' label in the custom-content picker. One imprecision in the claim: getGoodsModifiers/getHistoryModifiers (economicGenerator.js:1248/1044) read GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER and SERVICE_TIER_DATA, not EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER, so the typo'd key is never on any institution-gate path at all — which is consistent with the claim's overall 'inert landmine' framing. Severity low is honest: no crash, no engine output change, but real data-integrity defects (undefined category, dead misnamed key) that bite any future consumer grouping by category or honoring institution gates.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is sound but two additions: (1) If adding TRADE: 'trade' to GOODS_CATEGORIES, also check src/domain/customContentSchema.js — its classification enum (comment at line 96) deliberately mirrors the six existing GOODS_CATEGORIES values, so adding a seventh may need a matching schema update; recategorizing the three records to an existing category avoids that ripple. (2) There is a fourth 'Enslaved persons' record at tradeGoodsData.js:594 inside GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.town using a third schema (institutions/tradeRoutes/resourceBoost/institutionBoost/routeBoost, no category/p/on) that getGoodsModifiers (economicGenerator.js:1247) can never select — h.on is undefined and _rng() < undefined is always false — so any cleanup of the slave-trade records should decide what that record is supposed to do too. Minor: renaming requiresInstitution → requiredInstitution is correct for schema consistency, but note EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER is not read by any institution-gating code today, so the rename is future-proofing, not a behavior change (records are on: false regardless).

verdicts.reasoning: Every element of the claim reproduces against the real modules. (1) GOODS_CATEGORIES (src/data/tradeGoodsData.js:10-17) has no TRADE member; a node import of the module confirms GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE === undefined and that exactly three records — EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town/city 'Enslaved persons' (:130, :202) and IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town 'Enslaved persons' (:389) — end up with category: undefined (the key vanishes under JSON.stringify). (2) Those export records use `requiresInstitution` (:135, :207) against a documented schema (:24) and two engine readers that resolve `h.requiredInstitution` (economicGenerator.js:1051-1052, 1254-1255), so the documented institution gate would silently never apply. (3) Failure path today is slightly stronger than claimed: besides EventComposer.jsx:262 (keys-only, inert), customRegistry.enumeratePrebuiltTradeGoods (src/lib/customRegistry.js:27, 225-253) materializes both tables and maps `props?.category || 'other'` into subcategory, which EntityPicker.jsx displays and searches (:78, :132, :221-223) — so 'Enslaved persons' is live in the picker today, misclassified as 'other'. (4) The trace also shows the wider broken seam: the generation engine never reads EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER (it reads GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER via getGoodsModifiers:1247 and SERVICE_TIER_DATA via getHistoryModifiers:1044), and the GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER 'Enslaved persons' record (tradeGoodsData.js:594-600) uses a third incompatible schema (institutions/tradeRoutes/*Boost, no p/on/category) whose reader skips it unconditionally (h.on undefined; even a forced toggle dies at _rng() < undefined). So the slave-trade good is unreachable in generation despite Slave market institutions being fully wired (institutionalCatalog.js:975, institutionServices.js:478, assembleInstitutions.js:166). Nothing in the gate catches this: validate:data is only a duplicate-key scan (package.json:9). Severity: medium — a real data-integrity defect and broken seam (three inconsistent schemas for the same content, dangling constant reference, typo'd gate key) with a small live user-visible manifestation, but no crash, no security/money impact, and default generated output unaffected since the records are on:false.
verdicts.fix_note: Renaming requiresInstitution → requiredInstitution at tradeGoodsData.js:135/:207 is correct but insufficient on its own. Corrections: (a) Do not define GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE in isolation — TradeDynamicsPanel.jsx CAT_COLORS (:9-16) and customContentSchema.js TRADE_CATEGORIES (§14) both enumerate only the six existing categories, so a new 'trade' value would render with the blank-label fallback and be missing from the custom-content taxonomy; either add it in all three places or reuse an existing category. (b) The IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town record (:389-392) has the third undefined-category reference — cover it too. (c) The fix as scoped still leaves the good unreachable in generation: the engine reads GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER, whose town/city 'Enslaved persons' records (:594-600 etc.) use an incompatible schema (institutions/tradeRoutes/*Boost, no p/on/requiredInstitution) that getGoodsModifiers (economicGenerator.js:1247-1264) cannot evaluate — normalize those records to the {category, p, on, requiredInstitution} shape if the content is meant to be live, noting the reader supports only a single requiredInstitution string while the record intends three alternative institutions. (d) Consider a cheap structural guard: validate:data only finds duplicate keys today; a data validator asserting every goods record's category is in Object.values(GOODS_CATEGORIES) and that no record carries unknown keys (e.g. requiresInstitution) would have caught all of this.

confirmed.title: GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.town['Enslaved persons'] is a boost-table record in a goods table — no p/on/desc, so it can never be emitted and renders as a malformed entry
confirmed.evidence: Every other GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER record has {category, p, on, desc, requiredInstitution?}. The town 'Enslaved persons' entry (lines 594-600) instead has {institutions, tradeRoutes, resourceBoost, institutionBoost, routeBoost} — the shape of a probability-modifier table, evidently pasted into the wrong table. In getGoodsModifiers (economicGenerator.js:1247-1264) `h.on` is undefined (falsy → skipped), and even with an explicit toggle, `_rng() < h.p` is `_rng() < undefined` → always false, so the good can never appear; any UI iterating this table shows an entry with undefined desc/category/p. Note the EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER copy of the same good (line 130-135) has the proper goods shape, confirming divergence between the duplicated tables.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the record with the goods-shaped version from EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town (p: 0.15, on: false, requiredInstitution: 'Slave market', desc), or move the boost-shaped record to whatever modifier table it was written for.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified directly in the source. GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.town['Enslaved persons'] (tradeGoodsData.js:594-600) is a boost-shaped record {institutions, tradeRoutes, resourceBoost, institutionBoost, routeBoost} in a table where every other record across all six tiers is goods-shaped {category, p, on, desc, requiredInstitution?}. The boost fields are consumed nowhere in the codebase (only an unrelated local var named routeBoost in institutionProbability.js:91), so the record is orphaned. In getGoodsModifiers (economicGenerator.js:1247-1264) — the function that actually produces settlement exports via generateTradeIncomeStreams — `h.on` is undefined (falsy, skipped) and even with an explicit toggle override `_rng() < h.p` is `_rng() < undefined` → always false, so the good can never be emitted, exactly as claimed. The properly-shaped twin in EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town (lines 130-136) confirms divergence between the duplicated tables. The other two consumers (institutionProbability.js getPriorityModifiers, structuralValidator.js _getPriorityModifiers) only read good.institutionKeywords, absent here, so they skip it without crashing. One sub-claim is overstated: no UI component imports GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER (the goods toggle panel iterates SERVICE_TIER_DATA), so nothing renders a malformed entry — the defect is engine-side dead data only. validate:data is just a duplicate-key scan, so no gate catches it. Real defect, minimal blast radius (a default-off rare good that can never appear even when its required institution exists): severity low is honest.
verdicts.fix_note: Two corrections to the suggested fix: (1) Do not copy the EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town record verbatim — it uses the field name `requiresInstitution` (tradeGoodsData.js:135), but getGoodsModifiers reads `h.requiredInstitution` (economicGenerator.js:1251-1254), so a verbatim copy would silently drop the Slave-market gating. Use `requiredInstitution: 'Slave market'` as the reviewer's fix text correctly shows (the same `requiresInstitution` misspelling also exists at EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER lines 135 and 207 and is its own latent seam). (2) The divergence is slightly broader than one record: GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.city has no 'Enslaved persons' entry at all while EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.city does (line 202), so the city-tier good is also unreachable in the engine. Also note: since the good is `on: false` by default and engine override keys are `${tier}_export_${name}` while the toggle UI writes `${tier}_good_${name}` keys, impact is limited to the good being permanently unavailable rather than visibly wrong on default runs.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED malformed record, verified at runtime against the real module. GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.town['Enslaved persons'] (src/data/tradeGoodsData.js:594-600) is {institutions, tradeRoutes, resourceBoost: 0, institutionBoost: 0.3, routeBoost: 0.15} while every other record in the table is {category, p, on, desc, requiredInstitution?}. A node one-liner importing the module confirms h.on === undefined, h.p === undefined, h.requiredInstitution === undefined.

Trace through the only three consumers of GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER (grep-verified: no others, no UI):
1. economicGenerator.js getGoodsModifiers (1247-1264), called from generateTradeIncomeStreams (1215) which produces the dossier's exports: the gate `(o[g] !== void 0 ? o[g] : h.on)` evaluates to undefined (falsy) by default -> entry skipped. Even with a truthy toggle at key `town_export_Enslaved persons`, reqInst is '' so it falls to `_rng() < h.p` = `_rng() < undefined` = false -> never pushed. The good is structurally unreachable, confirmed by execution.
2/3. structuralValidator.js:155 and institutionProbability.js:10 only read good.institutionKeywords (absent) -> no effect, no crash.

The boost fields (institutionBoost/routeBoost/resourceBoost) are read by NO code anywhere in src (grep-verified), and the named institutions ('Slave market', 'Slave market district', 'Human trafficking network') are real entries in institutionalCatalog.js/institutionServices.js — so this record encodes a designed conditional mechanic (boost p when slave-market institutions exist, +boost on port/crossroads/river) that was never wired, pasted into a goods-record slot. EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town['Enslaved persons'] (lines 130-135) confirms the intended goods shape, proving divergence between the duplicated tables.

Why LOW, not higher — the practical delta is near zero on every reachable path:
(a) The intended twin entry is on:false (dark content off by default), so default generation output is IDENTICAL with or without the bug.
(b) There is no UI path to enable it anyway: the goods toggle panel (TradeDynamicsPanel.jsx:22-38) iterates SERVICE_TIER_DATA (servicesGenerator.js:491), which contains no 'Enslaved persons' entry; and getGoodsModifiers reads `${tier}_export_` toggle keys while the UI writes `${tier}_good_` keys.
(c) The claim's UI consequence ('any UI iterating this table shows a malformed entry') is FALSE — no UI iterates GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.
(d) Slave-trade content still reaches the dossier via an independent emergent path (economicGenerator.js:2186-2213: tier/criminal/occupied-driven roll pushing 'Slave trade —…' exports and 'Enslaved labour —…' imports), so the simulation does not silently lose the theme.
(e) No crash, no determinism impact (the skipped entry consumes no rng draw either way, consistently).

Net: a real data-integrity defect and unfinished seam (dead designed mechanic, duplicated-table divergence), but with no observable wrong output on any path a user can reach today.
verdicts.fix_note: Do NOT just copy the EXPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town entry (lines 130-135) as the claim implies — that twin has two latent defects of its own: (1) `category: GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE` is undefined (GOODS_CATEGORIES at tradeGoodsData.js:10-17 has no TRADE member; runtime-verified undefined), and (2) the key is misspelled `requiresInstitution` (lines 135, 207) where getGoodsModifiers reads `h.requiredInstitution` (economicGenerator.js:1254) — copying it verbatim would make the good emittable WITHOUT a slave market once toggled on, which is worse than the current dead entry. A correct fix: give line 594 the goods shape {category: <real category>, p: 0.15, on: false, desc, requiredInstitution: 'Slave market'}, fix `requiresInstitution`→`requiredInstitution` and the undefined TRADE category at lines 131/203/390 too, and decide whether GOODS_MODIFIERS_BY_TIER.city should gain the missing 'Enslaved persons' counterpart (EXPORT table has one at line 202; MODIFIERS table does not — runtime-verified). Also note the fix alone still leaves the good user-unreachable: the toggle UI iterates SERVICE_TIER_DATA (no such entry) and writes `_good_` keys while getGoodsModifiers reads `_export_` keys — surfacing it requires wiring on that seam as well. If the boost mechanic (institutionBoost/routeBoost) was the actual intent, it needs new consuming code in getGoodsModifiers, not just a data reshape.

confirmed.title: IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town has an object entry ('Enslaved persons') mixed among array buckets; table appears to be dead data drifting from its in-generator duplicate
confirmed.evidence: IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town (line 388) contains `'Enslaved persons': { category, desc }` (an object, missing name/on) sitting beside `fromCityOrMetropolis`/`fromHinterland` array buckets — any consumer iterating buckets as arrays (the pattern getUpgradeChain uses on its duplicate) would break or mis-handle it. grep finds no consumer of IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER anywhere in materialized src; the generator instead carries a verbatim private duplicate UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER (economicGenerator.js:1267-1478) WITHOUT this entry — classic duplicated-table drift from the strangler-fig migration.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Delete IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER or make it the single source consumed by getUpgradeChain; if kept, move 'Enslaved persons' into a properly-shaped bucket entry.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified at src/data/tradeGoodsData.js:388-392: IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER.town contains an object-map entry ('Enslaved persons': {category, desc}, no name/on) beside array buckets — and additionally references GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE, which does not exist in the enum (lines 10-17), so category is undefined. Verified at src/generators/economicGenerator.js:1267-1478 that UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER is a verbatim private duplicate of IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER WITHOUT the 'Enslaved persons' entry, and getUpgradeChain (1481-1502) reads only the duplicate and only iterates bucket arrays — so the data-file entry has zero generation effect; the duplicated-table drift is real. One evidence detail is wrong: the table is NOT dead — src/lib/customRegistry.js:27,253 ingests it for the Compendium trade-goods registry. However that consumer iterates the second level as name→props (the EXPORT shape), so it mis-ingests the array buckets (creates junk entries named 'basic', 'fromHigher', 'fromCityOrMetropolis', 'fromHinterland', 'fromMetropolis' and never registers the actual import goods like Salt/Metal tools), while the object-shaped 'Enslaved persons' entry is ironically the only import entry it handles correctly. The substance of the finding — mixed-shape table plus drifting in-generator duplicate is a real unfinished seam with no functioning single source of truth — holds; the consumer story is inverted, and the real consumer makes it worse, not better. Severity low is fair for the cited defect itself: no crash, no money/security, generation unaffected on the main path; the adjacent customRegistry mis-ingestion (junk picker entries, missing real import goods) is a related but distinct medium-low bug. Confidence ~0.85.
verdicts.fix_note: Do NOT simply delete IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER — customRegistry.js (lines 27, 253) imports and ingests it; deletion breaks the Compendium registry build. Correct fix: (1) normalize the 'Enslaved persons' entry into a properly-shaped item inside a bucket array (with name/on), or remove it if towns importing slaves was never wired up; (2) make IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER the single source consumed by getUpgradeChain and delete the private UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER duplicate in economicGenerator.js:1267-1478; (3) fix customRegistry's ingest of the import table — it currently assumes the EXPORT name→props shape and mis-ingests the bucket→array shape, registering bucket keys ('basic', 'fromCityOrMetropolis', etc.) as trade goods while skipping the real goods; (4) GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE does not exist (also referenced at tradeGoodsData.js:131 and :203 in the export table) — add a TRADE key or use an existing category, otherwise category is undefined on every 'Enslaved persons' entry.

verdicts.reasoning: The anomaly at src/data/tradeGoodsData.js:389 is real and the duplicated-table drift is confirmed (economicGenerator.js:1267 private UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER, consumed by getUpgradeChain at :1481, lacks the entry; the engine never reads the exported table). However, two key parts of the claim are wrong, and the real failure is worse than 'dead data': (1) IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER is NOT unconsumed — src/lib/customRegistry.js:253 ingests it into the registry behind CompendiumPanel.jsx:533, EntityPicker.jsx:46/68, dependencyEngine.js:120, and inferSupplyChains.js:53. (2) The failure mode is inverted: the consumer iterates tier→name→props (EXPORT's shape), not buckets-as-arrays. Demonstrated by running buildRegistry({}) against the real modules: the prebuilt tradeGoods list contains five phantom goods literally named 'basic', 'fromHigher', 'fromCityOrMetropolis', 'fromHinterland', 'fromMetropolis' (direction import, empty desc, subcategory 'other'), every real import-only good (Salt, Metal tools, Cloth, Luxury textiles, Timber, etc.) is entirely absent from the registry (Salt resolves to nothing, breaking dependency validation for refs to import goods), and 'Enslaved persons' is ironically the only import entry handled in the intended shape — surfacing ungated in the picker, unlike its export siblings (lines 130/202) which are gated with on:false + requiresInstitution. Bonus defect confirmed at runtime: GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE does not exist, so category is undefined at lines 131, 203, and 390. Severity medium: broken seam and garbage/missing entries in a real UI feature (Compendium/EntityPicker) plus genuine duplicated-table drift, but no impact on core simulation output, dossier/PDF, billing, or determinism, and no crash (props?.category tolerates the bad shapes).
verdicts.fix_note: The implied fix (delete the stray entry / treat the table as dead) is wrong on both counts. The table is live — customRegistry.js:253 consumes it — so do not delete it; and removing only the 'Enslaved persons' object leaves the actual bug, which is the shape mismatch between IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER (tier→bucket→array) and customRegistry's ingest (assumes EXPORT's tier→name→props). Correct fix: either (a) normalize IMPORT_GOODS_BY_TIER to the tier→name→props shape (preferred — also enables single-sourcing economicGenerator's UPGRADE_GOODS_BY_TIER from it, or at least a parity test, killing the drift class), or (b) make customRegistry's ingest detect Array.isArray(props) buckets and iterate items by item.name. If 'Enslaved persons' is intended as a town import, give it the gated shape of its export siblings (on:false, p, requiresInstitution) inside a proper bucket. Separately fix GOODS_CATEGORIES.TRADE being undefined at tradeGoodsData.js:131/203/390 (add a TRADE category or reuse an existing one).

confirmed.title: User-visible income-source text corrupted by bad find/replace: 'weavers and fullers convert SEVERITY to cloth'
confirmed.evidence: The 'Wool & Textile Trade' income source desc reads 'Local flocks provide raw wool; weavers and fullers convert SEVERITY to cloth sold across the region.' (line 2019). 'SEVERITY' is the name of an imported constant (line 21) — a refactor/rename clearly clobbered a word ('it'/'fleece') inside a string literal. This desc is rendered in the dossier's income sources for any settlement with grazing_land + weaver/fuller institutions.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Restore the intended word, e.g. '...weavers and fullers convert it to cloth sold across the region.'

verdicts.reasoning: Confirmed verbatim. src/generators/economicGenerator.js:2019 contains the user-facing string 'Local flocks provide raw wool; weavers and fullers convert SEVERITY to cloth sold across the region.' — nonsense English where the literal token SEVERITY (the constant imported at line 21) replaced a word inside the string. The corruption mechanism is consistent with the file's history: the surrounding code retains minified identifiers (ee, E, K, V, de), and a whole-word find/replace of a minified identifier ('it') to SEVERITY during deobfuscation would turn 'convert it to cloth' into exactly this text; a grep confirms this is the only string literal hit by the rename. The path is live, not dead code: the 'Wool & Textile Trade' branch (lines 2006-2021) fires when localProduction/exports mention wool/fleece/cloth/textile, an institution name contains weav/fuller/cloth, no prior wool/textile income exists, and config.nearbyResources includes grazing_land — a real resource key (src/data/resourceData.js:194, referenced throughout src/data/economicData.js). The desc survives normalization via object spreads (incomeBuild → incomeWeighted → incomeNormalized, lines 2098-2104) into economy.incomeSources (line 2532), and is rendered on-screen in the dossier's Income Sources section (src/components/new/tabs/EconomicsTab.jsx:269, `src.desc`) and carried into the PDF view model (src/pdf/lib/viewModel.js:535, normalizeIncomeSources spreads `...s` preserving desc). No guard strips or rewrites the text, and nothing suggests it is intentional. Severity 'low' is honest: cosmetic corruption of user-visible prose on a conditional path; no determinism, data-integrity, or billing impact.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct: restore 'convert it to cloth' (the original word was almost certainly 'it', clobbered by a whole-word rename of a minified identifier to SEVERITY). A repo-wide grep found no other string literals corrupted by the same rename, so the one-line fix is complete.

verdicts.reasoning: Confirmed and reproduced end-to-end. The string at src/generators/economicGenerator.js:2019 literally contains 'convert SEVERITY to cloth' — SEVERITY is the imported constant name (line 21) clobbered into a string literal by a bad find/replace (git log -L shows the corruption predates the recent reformat; it existed in the earlier minified form). The branch is reachable on a normal path: a node one-liner calling the real exported generateEconomicState('town', [Weaver's Hall, Fuller workshop, Market Square], 'crossroads', {}, {nearbyResources:['grazing_land']}) emits incomeSources containing exactly desc='Local flocks provide raw wool; weavers and fullers convert SEVERITY to cloth sold across the region.' This flows into settlement.economics.incomeSources (line 2532) via the active pipeline step src/generators/steps/generateEconomy.js:27, and is rendered verbatim in both the on-screen dossier (src/components/new/tabs/EconomicsTab.jsx:269 renders src.desc) and the exported PDF (src/pdf/sections/EconomicsTrade.jsx:68-72 renders s.desc; pdf/lib/viewModel.js normalizeIncomeSources spreads ...s preserving desc). So any pastoral settlement with weaver/fuller/cloth institutions and grazing_land shows garbled text in both outputs. No crash, no data corruption beyond the prose, no determinism or parity impact (both renderers consistently show the same corrupted string).
verdicts.fix_note: Fix is a one-word string edit at economicGenerator.js:2019: replace 'SEVERITY' with 'it' (reads naturally: 'weavers and fullers convert it to cloth') — 'fleece' also fits and matches the keyword list on line 2007. Do NOT touch the import on line 21; SEVERITY is genuinely used elsewhere in the file (lines 101-103, 246, 318, 2580, etc.). One correction to the claim's framing: since SEVERITY appears inside a string literal, there is no undefined-at-runtime or constant-resolution issue — it is purely corrupted user-facing prose, which is why no test or typecheck caught it. Worth also grepping the repo for other ALL_CAPS constant names embedded inside string literals (e.g. TIER_ORDER) in case the same find/replace clobbered other prose; a quick check of this file found no other instances.

confirmed.title: River Tolls income matches bare 'dock' — 'Airship docking' counts as river dock, contradicting the adjacent comment that fixed the port branch for exactly this
confirmed.evidence: The comment at lines 1742-1745 states Port Duties keys on 'docks/port' specifically so "'Airship docking' never reads as a harbour", and the port branch (line 1746) was fixed accordingly. But the river branch right below (line 1752) still uses `hasInst('dock', 'port facilit') && tradeRoute === 'river'` — `'airship docking'.includes('dock')` is true, so a river-route settlement whose only 'dock' is an airship dock collects River Tolls ('Tolls on river traffic, dock fees, and ferry rights') attributed to nonexistent river infrastructure.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Use the same guarded pattern as the port branch, e.g. hasInst('docks/port', 'river dock', 'boatyard', 'ferry') or exclude names containing 'airship'.

verdicts.reasoning: Empirically reproduced: generateEconomicState('metropolis', [{name:'Airship docking (high magic)'}], 'river') emits a 'River Tolls' income source, while the same institution on a 'port' route correctly yields no 'Port Duties'. The hasInst helper (economicGenerator.js:1679) does lowercase substring matching, so 'airship docking (high magic)' matches the bare 'dock' keyword at line 1752. The comment at 1742-1745 explicitly documents the intent that 'Airship docking' must never read as harbour infrastructure — the port branch (1746) was guarded accordingly, but the river branch kept the pre-fix pattern. Reachable: 'Airship docking (high magic)' (institutionalCatalog.js:2056, minTier metropolis, no trade-route restriction) can roll on a river-route metropolis while the optional 'Docks/port facilities' (baseChance 0.55-0.6, required:false) does not. A catalog scan shows the only institution names containing 'dock' are 'Docks/port facilities' and 'Airship docking (high magic)', so the bare 'dock' keyword is redundant with 'port facilit' except for the airship false positive. tests/joins/ports.test.js:214-221 guards the port branch against airship but has no river-route equivalent. Not dead code, no upstream guard, contradicted by the adjacent intent comment — the finding holds.
verdicts.fix_note: The minimal correct fix is to drop the redundant bare 'dock' keyword: hasInst('port facilit') (or 'docks/port') already matches the only real dock institution, 'Docks/port facilities'. Note the suggested keys in the report are partly wrong: there is no 'river dock' institution in the catalog; river-flavored institutions are 'River boatyard', 'River ferry', and 'Barge and river transport company' (institutionalCatalog.js:474, 483, 1544). Whether those should also qualify for River Tolls (the desc mentions 'ferry rights') is a design decision beyond the bug fix. Also remember hasInst lowercases institution names but not keywords, so any added keywords must be lowercase. A river-route regression test mirroring ports.test.js:214-221 ('Airship docking on a river route earns no River Tolls') would close the untested seam.

verdicts.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against the real modules. The river branch at src/generators/economicGenerator.js:1752 uses hasInst('dock', 'port facilit'); hasInst (line 1679) is a lowercase substring match over institution names, so 'Airship docking (high magic)' matches the bare 'dock' keyword. The comment directly above (lines 1742-1745) documents that the port branch was deliberately tightened to 'docks/port' so 'Airship docking' never reads as a harbour — the river branch was not given the same treatment. The failure state is reachable through ordinary config: 'Airship docking (high magic)' (institutionalCatalog.js:2056, metropolis, baseChance 0.1, x1.8 exotic boost at priorityMagic>=66 per institutionProbability.js:160-176) has no trade-route gate, while the genuine 'Docks/port facilities' is baseChance 0.55-0.6 on river routes and fails its roll ~40% of the time; structuralValidator.js only records violations and never removes institutions. A node one-liner calling generateEconomicState('metropolis', [Airship docking], 'river', ...) produces {"source":"River Tolls","percentage":36,"desc":"Tolls on river traffic, dock fees, and ferry rights."} while the identical settlement without the airship dock produces no river income — so the airship dock alone fabricates a major civic income line attributed to nonexistent river docks/ferries, shown in both dossier and PDF. Wrong-but-plausible simulation output on a narrow configuration (river-route metropolis with magic>=66, ~7% of such rolls): a real coherence defect, edge-case in reach, no crash/money/security impact.
verdicts.fix_note: The minimal fix is to drop only the bare 'dock' keyword: change hasInst('dock', 'port facilit') to hasInst('docks/port', 'port facilit') (or just hasInst('docks/port'), since the sole legitimate match 'Docks/port facilities' lowercases to 'docks/port facilities' and contains both keys). Verified against the catalog that no other institution matches: 'River ferry', 'River boatyard', and 'Barge and river transport company' contain neither keyword and already do not trigger the branch. Note the branch desc promises 'ferry rights' yet a settlement whose only river institution is 'River ferry' gets no River Tolls — if desired, adding 'river ferry'/'barge' as additional keys would make the line cohere better, but that is a behavior expansion beyond this bug, and any keyword change shifts income normalization percentages so seeded snapshot tests may need regeneration.

confirmed.title: resolveConfig: noMagic=true while priorityMagicEffective=50 when config.priorityMagic is undefined — contradictory magic signals
confirmed.evidence: `priorityMagicEffective = config.magicExists === false ? 0 : (basePriorityMagic ?? 50)` (lines 66-67) defaults an UNSET priorityMagic to 50 (magic moderately present), but `noMagic = config.magicExists === false || (basePriorityMagic || 0) === 0` (line 86) treats the same unset value as 0 → noMagic = true. A config missing priorityMagic (older saves, API-built configs) then gets isolated routes filtered/rewritten to road on no-magic grounds (lines 105, 119) and townPlus+isolated traces claiming 'no-magic forces road access', while the rest of the pipeline (magicLevel, chain magic substitution, magic income) runs at priority 50.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Compute noMagic from the same resolved value: `const noMagic = config.magicExists === false || priorityMagicEffective === 0;`.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified empirically: running generateSettlementPipeline({ settType: 'town', tradeRouteAccess: 'isolated' }) headless yields noMagic=true alongside priorityMagicEffective=50, magicLevel='medium', effectiveConfig.priorityMagic=50, with the explicit 'isolated' route rewritten to 'road' and a trace claiming 'town-plus + no-magic forces road access' — false, since the rest of the pipeline runs at medium magic. The path is reachable: generateSettlementPipeline.js:41-56 does no config normalization; store persist (store/index.js:61-82) has no merge/migrate so old saves lacking the key stay lacking; and tests/property/pipeline.property.test.js:33-39 fuzzes configs with no priorityMagic at all, including isolated+townPlus combos. The unset-means-zero reading at resolveConfig.js:86 contradicts not only line 66-67 in the same function but also the codebase's two other no-magic checks (magicFilter.js:43 uses `?? 50`; servicesGenerator.js:1964 uses `|| 50`), so it is not intentional semantics. No test pins line 86's behavior. Impact is contained to non-wizard configs (the UI defaults priorityMagic to 50), manifesting as a wrongly filtered route pool, a silently overridden explicit user choice, and a false causal trace.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct and matches the canonical semantics in magicFilter.js:43. Two notes: (1) since priorityMagicEffective is already forced to 0 when magicExists === false (line 66-67), the fix simplifies to `const noMagic = priorityMagicEffective === 0;` — keeping the explicit magicExists clause is harmless but redundant. (2) The fix changes rng pool composition under tradeRouteAccess='random_trade' for configs lacking priorityMagic (isolated is no longer filtered out), so route outcomes for existing seed+config combos in that category will shift — check whether any snapshot tests use such minimal configs before landing.

verdicts.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against the real modules. generateSettlementPipeline({settType:'city', tradeRouteAccess:'isolated'}, null, {seed:12345}) yields the exact contradiction claimed: noMagic=true (src/generators/steps/resolveConfig.js:86, `(basePriorityMagic || 0) === 0` maps undefined to 0) while priorityMagicEffective=50 and magicLevel='medium' (lines 66-67, `basePriorityMagic ?? 50`). Consequences verified: the explicit 'isolated' route is silently rewritten to 'road' (line 119), and a false trace receipt is recorded — "Explicit 'isolated' chosen, but town-plus + no-magic forces road access" — in a world the same step resolves as magic-medium; that trace feeds the PipelineRail and AI-grounding layers. A contrast run with priorityMagic:50 explicitly present (the documented default `??` is supposed to supply) produces a different settlement (noMagic=false, tradeRoute='isolated'), proving missing-key != documented-default, an input-equivalence break. Every sibling consumer treats unset as 50: priorityHelpers.js:100, servicesGenerator.js:1964, magicFilter.js:43, economicGenerator.js:2269, and the migrateConfig helpers in SettlementsPanel.jsx:35 / SettlementDetail.jsx:208 — resolveConfig:86 is the lone outlier. Severity caveat: I traced all in-app paths and none currently deliver an undefined priorityMagic — DEFAULT_CONFIG has carried priorityMagic:50 since the initial commit (git show e608542), effectiveConfig snapshots always set it (line 152) covering the applyChange fallback (settlementSlice.js:847-849), samples define it, and default randomSliderMode rolls 5-95. The trigger requires a headless/programmatic caller with a minimal config — a surface the codebase explicitly supports ("snapshot tests, scripts, server jobs", store/index.js:90-95; pipeline signature `config = {}`). Real internal-coherence defect with demonstrated wrong output and false trace receipts, but not reachable from the shipped UI today, so medium rather than high.
verdicts.fix_note: The right fix is `const noMagic = config.magicExists === false || priorityMagicEffective === 0;` — reuse the already-resolved value from lines 66-67 rather than re-deriving it; it is 0 exactly when magicExists===false or the (defaulted) priority is 0, matching priorityHelpers.js:100 semantics exactly. If re-deriving instead, use `(basePriorityMagic ?? 50) === 0`, NOT `(basePriorityMagic || 50) === 0`: `||` would coerce an explicit null to 50 here while line 67's `??` coerces null to 50 too — actually consistent — but `||` would also turn an explicit 0 into 50, breaking the explicit no-magic-slider case, so `??` is required. Two follow-on cautions: (1) snapshot/property tests that feed minimal configs (no priorityMagic) will see different settlements after the fix, since the route pool changes alter rng draw order downstream — UI-originated configs are unaffected because they always carry the key; (2) note the same `?? 0` vs `?? 50` divergence exists at neighbourGenerator.js:148 (`(config.priorityMagic ?? 0) / 100`), which treats an unset priority as no-magic for neighbour magic level — worth auditing in the same pass, though it is a separate call path.

confirmed.title: sortBySeverity comparator yields NaN for the 'warning'/'note' severities that generateEconomicViability itself pushes
confirmed.evidence: sortBySeverity (lines 2579-2582) maps only SEVERITY.CRITICAL/IMPLAUSIBLE/DEPENDENCY/INEFFICIENCY. generateEconomicViability pushes issues with `severity: 'warning'` (line 2642) and warnings with `severity: 'note'` (line 2654); buildFactionList pushes stress issues with raw 'critical'/'implausible' strings (line 644). For unmapped severities `s[o.severity] - s[d.severity]` is NaN, making the sort order implementation-defined for those entries — the viability list ordering (critical-first) silently breaks whenever siege/isolation dependency issues are present.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add 'warning' and 'note' (and raw-string fallbacks) to the rank map, or default unknown severities with `?? 4`.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified: sortBySeverity (src/generators/economicGenerator.js:2579-2582) maps only the four SEVERITY constants ('critical'/'implausible'/'dependency'/'inefficiency', src/data/constants.js:16-21), while generateEconomicViability itself pushes severity:'warning' into issues (line 2642) and severity:'note' into warnings (line 2654, surviving the structuralWarnings filter at 2668), and both arrays go through sortBySeverity (2672-2673). The comparator returns NaN for those entries, making it an inconsistent comparator whose order is implementation-defined per spec — that core claim is true. However, two parts of the evidence are wrong or overstated: (1) buildFactionList line 644's raw 'critical'/'implausible' strings are exactly the SEVERITY constant values, so they rank fine; (2) the claimed impact ("critical-first ordering silently breaks") does not occur in practice — ES2023 SortCompare coerces NaN to +0 (equal), sort is stable, and the 'warning'/'note' entries are always the final pushes into their arrays, so on every mainstream engine they simply stay at the tail while mapped entries sort correctly. Critical-first ordering holds for all real consumers (simulationSpine.js:145 issues[0], pdf viewModel viabilitySlice); ViabilityTab.jsx re-buckets/re-sorts independently. So: real latent comparator defect (fragile seam — any future non-tail unmapped push would scramble order), but benign today. Low severity is correct.
verdicts.fix_note: Drop the part of the fix about line 644 raw-string fallbacks — 'critical'/'implausible' already equal the SEVERITY constant values and rank correctly. The needed fix is just explicit ranks for 'warning' and 'note' (deciding intentionally where they belong, e.g., 'warning' between dependency and inefficiency, 'note' last) plus a default for unknowns, e.g. `(s[o.severity] ?? 9) - (s[d.severity] ?? 9)`, so the comparator is always consistent.

verdicts.reasoning: Reproduced against the real module. sortBySeverity (economicGenerator.js:2579-2582) ranks only the four SEVERITY constants ('critical','implausible','dependency','inefficiency' per data/constants.js:16-21). generateEconomicViability itself pushes severity:'warning' into issues (line 2642, triggered whenever tradeDependencies contain a 'critical' dep — which happens for any isolated-route or under-siege settlement with import-dependent institutions, see lines 2241-2254) and severity:'note' into warnings (line 2654, ≥3 vulnerable deps; 'note' !== 'dependency' so it lands in structuralWarnings and is sorted at line 2673). For those entries the comparator returns NaN, making the comparator inconsistent; a node run importing the real sortBySeverity shows [ineff, warning, critical, implausible] comes back completely unsorted (critical NOT first), while a control array without unmapped severities sorts correctly. Consequence: the PDF renders viabilityIssues in this array order (pdf/lib/viewModel.js:547 → pdf/sections/EconomicsTrade.jsx:363), so the 'ACTIVE ECONOMIC ISSUES' list loses critical-first ordering exactly when siege/isolation dependency issues are present; it is also dossier/PDF parity drift because the on-screen ViabilityTab re-sorts by title itself and masks the broken order. No crash, no wrong simulation data, viable flag and counts unaffected — a real ordering/parity defect on a common config, hence medium. One evidence correction: the claim about line 644 using raw 'critical'/'implausible' strings is not a defect — those strings are identical to the SEVERITY constant values and map fine; the bug rests solely on 'warning' and 'note'.
verdicts.fix_note: Minimal correct fix: give unmapped severities a finite rank in the comparator, e.g. const rank = (x) => s[x.severity] ?? 4; return r.sort((o, d) => rank(o) - rank(d)); — or explicitly add 'warning' and 'note' to the map (there are no SEVERITY.WARNING/NOTE constants, so either add them to data/constants.js or rank the raw strings). Do NOT 'fix' line 644: 'critical'/'implausible' there already equal SEVERITY.CRITICAL/SEVERITY.IMPLAUSIBLE and sort correctly (though switching to the constants for consistency is harmless). Decide deliberately where 'warning'/'note' rank (probably after 'implausible'/before or after 'inefficiency') rather than letting them float; a deterministic fallback rank also protects against future unmapped severities reintroducing the inconsistent-comparator problem. Note sortBySeverity mutates its argument in place (Array.prototype.sort) — fine for current call sites which pass locally built arrays, but worth keeping in mind if the fix touches call sites.

confirmed.title: TERRAIN_DATA allowedResources mixes two id vocabularies: mountain/desert use snake_case RESOURCE_DATA-style keys, all other terrains use plain display words
confirmed.evidence: mountain.allowedResources are snake_case keys ('iron_deposits', 'stone_quarry', 'hunting_grounds', 'ancient_ruins' — lines 213-225) matching the RESOURCE_DATA key vocabulary used throughout the generators (RARE_RESOURCES, DEPLETED_IMPORT_MAP, RESOURCE_EXTRACTION_GATES), and desert likewise (lines 610-620). But coastal/riverside/forest/plains/hills use plain words ('fish', 'salt', 'timber', 'grain', 'iron ore' — lines 54-66, 131-142, 412-424, 517-529). Any consumer that joins allowedResources against resource keys works for two terrains and silently fails for five (or vice versa). No materialized consumer reads allowedResources, so this is either dead data or a break inside a stripped consumer — either way the table is internally inconsistent.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Normalize all seven terrains to the RESOURCE_DATA key vocabulary (or delete the field if it is dead).

verdicts.reasoning: The headline defect is confirmed: TERRAIN_DATA.allowedResources in src/data/geographyData.js mixes two id vocabularies — mountain (lines 213-225) and desert (lines 610-620) use snake_case RESOURCE_DATA-style keys (iron_deposits, stone_quarry, oasis_water, camel_herds), while coastal/riverside/forest/plains/hills use plain display words (fish, timber, grain, 'iron ore'). However, two evidence claims are wrong. First, the field is NOT dead: generateNarratives.js:33-40 (live pipeline step 14) passes allowedResources.slice(0,7) into generateResourceAnalysis and generateEconomicViability, and the resulting resourceAnalysis is rendered in the dossier (ResourcesTab/EconomicsTab/OverviewTab/DefenseTab) and the PDF (pdf/lib/viewModel.js:328/502/722). Second, the join does not 'work for two terrains and fail for five': RESOURCE_CHAINS in resourceData.js deliberately mirrors the mix — a commented 'Terrain-specific chains' section defines snake_case rawResource values exactly matching the mountain/desert tokens, so every terrain activates some chains. The mix nonetheless causes real cross-vocabulary misses in resourceGenerator.js:19-21 and 65-66: mountain's iron_deposits never matches the 'iron ore' chain, gemstone_deposits never matches 'gemstones', precious_metals never matches 'gold/silver ore', and stone_quarry fails the exact-presence check vs 'stone'. Result: a mountain settlement whose own economicStrengths are 'Mining/Stoneworking/Gemcutting' can never show active iron/stone/gem/precious-metal chains (hills can), buildViabilityReport can emit the incoherent critical import 'iron ore ... not available in Mountain terrain', and the dossier/PDF display raw snake_case strings (alpine_pasture, camel_herds) for mountain/desert while other terrains show clean words. This is a real internal-inconsistency defect with visible narrative-layer consequences, but it does not corrupt the core economy, money, auth, or determinism — severity low is honest.
verdicts.fix_note: Two corrections to the suggested fix. (1) Do not delete the field — it is live, not dead: resourceGenerator.js:19 calls terrain.allowedResources.some(...) unguarded and would throw, and generateNarratives.js step 14 feeds it into the rendered resourceAnalysis. (2) Normalizing geographyData.js alone is insufficient and would make things worse: RESOURCE_CHAINS.rawResource in resourceData.js mirrors the same mixed vocabulary (plain words for the 14 generic chains, snake_case for the 7 'Terrain-specific chains'), and evaluateEconomicActivity requires an exact nearbyResources.includes(chain.rawResource) match. Converting the five plain-word terrains to snake_case keys without simultaneously converting the generic chains' rawResource values would zero out all active chains for those terrains. Normalize both tables (geographyData.js allowedResources and resourceData.js RESOURCE_CHAINS.rawResource) to the RESOURCE_DATA key vocabulary in one pass, and add display-name mapping where the dossier renders chain.rawResource (ResourcesTab.jsx renders it verbatim, currently showing raw snake_case like 'alpine_pasture' for mountain/desert). Also note mountain's hot_springs_mineral sits at index 7 and is silently dropped by the slice(0,7) in generateNarratives.js:33 — worth fixing in the same pass.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED, and it is worse than claimed: the claim's statement "No materialized consumer reads allowedResources" is factually wrong — there are two live consumers, and the mixed vocabulary produces wrong, user-visible simulation output on both the web dossier and the PDF.

Vocabulary mix (verified): mountain (src/data/geographyData.js:213-224) and desert (:610-619) use snake_case RESOURCE_DATA-style keys; coastal/riverside/forest/plains/hills use plain display words. The snake_case entries are partly deliberate — they pair exactly with terrain-specific RESOURCE_CHAINS added at src/data/resourceData.js:546-600 (alpine_pasture, mountain_timber, desert_salt, glass_sand, camel_herds, oasis_water, hot_springs_mineral). The breakage is where mountain/desert snake_case entries must join against the original plain-word chains: 'iron_deposits' vs rawResource 'iron ore', 'precious_metals' vs 'gold/silver ore', 'gemstone_deposits' vs 'gemstones', 'stone_quarry' vs exact 'stone', 'coal_deposits' vs nothing.

Concrete failure path (every mountain settlement, pipeline step 14): src/generators/steps/generateNarratives.js:33-35 passes TERRAIN_DATA[terrain].allowedResources.slice(0,7) as nearbyResources into generateResourceAnalysis. In src/generators/resourceGenerator.js:19-20, chain activation requires substring match against allowedResources AND exact membership in nearbyResources. I ran the real modules (node ESM one-liner) across all seven terrains:
- mountain activeChains = only [alpine_pasture, mountain_timber]. The mining chains (iron ore 'very high' export, stone, gemstones, gold/silver, copper) can NEVER activate on mountain — the one terrain whose own economicStrengths say "Mining, Stoneworking, Gemcutting" and strategicValue says "mineral wealth". hills, by contrast, activates 5 chains including iron ore and stone because its allowedResources are plain words.
- buildViabilityReport (resourceGenerator.js:62-73) with a Smelter or Mine institution present yields imports.critical containing 'iron ore' with reason "Required by Mine but not available in Mountain terrain" (demonstrated output), plus 'gold/silver ore' likewise — while the same settlement's nearby-resource list shows "Iron Ore Deposits" as present. Mine/Smelter are real catalog institutions (src/data/institutionalCatalog.js:579, :1105) and RESOURCE_DATA.iron_deposits.instBoosts = {mine: 2.5, smelter: 2.2} (resourceData.js:221) makes exactly this combination likely on mountain terrain. Same for desert + precious_metals/gemstone_deposits.

User-visible surfaces: exploitation buckets render in src/components/new/tabs/ResourcesTab.jsx (Resource Exploitation section, same tab that prints "Mining/Gemcutting" strengths — an in-page contradiction) and PDF src/pdf/lib/viewModel.js resourcesSlice (~:721-755); imports.critical renders in src/components/new/tabs/EconomicsTab.jsx:193-194 and PDF src/pdf/sections/ResourcesProduction.jsx:38. This is a direct coherence break in the product's core promise (internally-coherent settlement), affecting every mountain settlement unconditionally (chain skew) and mountain/desert settlements with mining institutions (false critical imports). Severity: high (wrong simulation output on a main path, not data loss/security/crash).
verdicts.fix_note: The claim's framing ("dead data or stripped consumer") needs correction: consumers exist (resourceGenerator.js:19,65 via generateNarratives.js:33-41), so the fix must preserve their working joins. Do NOT simply rename mountain/desert allowedResources to plain display words — the snake_case entries are load-bearing exact-match pairs with the terrain-specific RESOURCE_CHAINS rawResource values (alpine_pasture, mountain_timber, desert_salt, glass_sand, camel_herds, oasis_water, hot_springs_mineral); renaming one side alone would break the currently-working mountain/desert chains. Minimal correct fix: make the classic mineral chains reachable from mountain/desert, either by adding the plain-word resources mountain genuinely has ('iron ore', 'stone', 'gemstones', 'gold/silver ore') to its allowedResources, or (better) replace the fragile substring/exact joins with an explicit alias map between RESOURCE_DATA keys and RESOURCE_CHAINS rawResource. Two traps for the fixer: (1) generateNarratives.js:33 slices allowedResources to the FIRST 7 entries before the exact-membership check, so appending new entries at the end does nothing — order matters; hot_springs_mineral already sits at index 7 of mountain's list, so the mineralHot chain is dead today for the same reason and should be fixed in the same pass. (2) buildViabilityReport's mustImport list is separate hardcoded data — mountain's "Charcoal and fuel" mustImport contradicts the mountain_timber chain's 'charcoal fuel' final product; adjacent, worth a follow-up. Any fix changes resourceAnalysis output, so expect snapshot/property test churn.

confirmed.title: Subsistence prosperity roll yields 70% Struggling / 30% Poor — matches neither of its own two documented distributions
confirmed.evidence: Comment at line 1161 says '35% chance of Struggling'; inline comment at 1163 says '40% Struggling, ~30% Poor, ~30% luckier'. The code `idx = _rng() < 0.4 ? 0 : _rng() < 0.5 ? 0 : 1` produces 0 with probability 0.4 + 0.6*0.5 = 0.7 and 1 with 0.3 — both branches of the middle case land on 0, and the 'luckier' outcome is impossible even before the `Math.min(1, idx)` cap. The prosperity label for every isolated thorp/hamlet is skewed: 70% Struggling instead of the documented 35-40%.
confirmed.suggested_fix: If the inline comment is the intent: `idx = _rng() < 0.4 ? 0 : 1` (40% Struggling, 60% Poor after the cap), or make the middle branch return 1.

verdicts.reasoning: Verified at src/generators/economicGenerator.js:1162-1164. The ternary `idx = _rng() < 0.4 ? 0 : _rng() < 0.5 ? 0 : 1` nests as `0.4 ? 0 : (0.5 ? 0 : 1)`, so both the 40% branch and the 60%*50%=30% middle branch yield 0; empirical Monte Carlo confirms P(idx=0)=0.700, P(idx=1)=0.300. This contradicts both adjacent comments (line 1161: '35% chance of Struggling'; line 1163 inline: '40% Struggling, ~30% Poor, ~30% luckier'). The path is live: line 2527 forces Z='Subsistence' for every isolated thorp/hamlet and line 2530 feeds it to generateEconomicNarrative, so all isolated small settlements get a 70%-Struggling base roll instead of the documented 35-40%. Strong typo evidence: the cap `Math.max(0, Math.min(1, idx))` on line 1164 is dead protection as written (idx can never exceed 1), which only makes sense if a third 'luckier' branch producing 2 was intended — classic chained-ternary copy-paste error where two branches return the same literal. No test pins this distribution (grep over tests/ shows prosperity labels only as fixture inputs), so it is not intentional-per-test.
