Since selection is runtime-only and never persisted, clearing on any wholesale mapState swap is safe and matches the documented intent that these fields mirror the currently-rendered placements.

confirmed.title: persistSaveUpdate re-throws inside fire-and-forget call sites in applyQueuedRegionalImpact/resolveRegionalImpact, producing unhandled promise rejections on save failure
confirmed.evidence: persistSaveUpdate (lines 154-160) catches the savesService.update failure, warns, then re-throws - returning a rejected promise. applyQueuedRegionalImpact calls it fire-and-forget with no .catch (lines 1108-1113), as does resolveRegionalImpact (lines 1172-1177). When the cloud save update fails (offline, RLS, expired session), the rejection is unhandled - the impact is already marked applied/resolved in local state and the campaign graph, the settlement mutation never reaches the cloud, and the only signal is an unhandled-rejection console error; no rollback or user-visible error occurs.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Either drop the re-throw in persistSaveUpdate for these fire-and-forget paths, or attach a .catch at the call sites that surfaces a toast/error state (and ideally marks the save as needing re-sync) instead of leaving the rejection unhandled while local and cloud state silently diverge.

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified against the actual code; the claim holds on every factual point.

1) persistSaveUpdate (campaignSlice.js:154-160) calls savesService.update(...).catch(e => { console.warn(...); throw e; }) — it logs then RE-THROWS, so it returns a rejected promise on failure rather than recovering.

2) savesService.update is supabaseUpdate when Supabase is configured (saves.js:327 -> 195-215), which does `if (error) throw error` (saves.js:214). Offline / RLS-denied / expired-session all yield a Supabase error -> rejected promise. (Unconfigured/local mode uses localUpdate which won't reject, but the production path is supabaseUpdate.)

3) Both call sites are genuinely fire-and-forget. applyQueuedRegionalImpact (campaignSlice.js:1108-1113) and resolveRegionalImpact (1172-1177) invoke persistSaveUpdate(...) as a bare statement — not awaited, no .catch — then return the synchronous `result`. The component callers consume only that synchronous value: SettlementsPanel.jsx:987-997 and RegionalImpactInbox.jsx:55-67 destructure `result` and never touch a promise. Contrast persistSaveUpdates (plural, 162-170) which IS awaited and for which the re-throw is meaningful — confirming the re-throw is only useful for awaited callers, which these two are not.

4) By the time persistSaveUpdate runs, set() has already mutated local state: impact marked applied/resolved via domainSetRegionalImpactStatus (1094 / 1158), savedSettlements replaced (1085 / 1149), persistCampaignState called (1097 / 1161). So on cloud-save failure the local state and campaign graph diverge from the cloud with no rollback.

5) The only catch is the global window 'unhandledrejection' handler (errorReporter.js:86-88) which forwards to telemetry — no toast, no error state, no re-sync flag, no rollback. On a later reload the cloud row still lacks the settlement mutation, so the impact silently re-appears un-applied. That is real, silent local/cloud divergence, exactly as described.

I could not refute it: the path is reachable from two live UI buttons, the cited APIs behave as claimed, no upstream guard recovers the rejection, and no nearby comment/test marks it intentional. Severity 'low' is honest — failure only under network/RLS/session-loss on a non-critical feature, no crash/money/security impact, but genuine edge-case data divergence.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is sound. Preferred: attach a .catch at the two fire-and-forget call sites (1108-1113, 1172-1177) that surfaces a user-visible error/toast and flags the save as needing re-sync, rather than only relying on the global unhandledrejection telemetry. Note: do NOT simply drop the re-throw in persistSaveUpdate, because persistSaveUpdates (the awaited plural helper, 162-170) relies on that rejection to propagate to its awaiting callers; removing the throw would silently weaken that path too. Cleaner to leave persistSaveUpdate as-is and add the .catch at the singular call sites (or introduce a fire-and-forget variant that handles its own rejection).

verdicts.reasoning:
Confirmed end-to-end. The campaignSlice variant of persistSaveUpdate (src/store/campaignSlice.js:154-160) catches the savesService.update failure, warns, then RE-THROWS, returning a rejected promise. This re-throw exists to serve the awaited batch helper persistSaveUpdates (162-170, awaited at 965/991/1021). But the two singular call sites do NOT await or .catch it: applyQueuedRegionalImpact (synchronous arrow at 1048; persist fired at 1109-1112) and resolveRegionalImpact (synchronous arrow at 1117; persist fired at 1173-1176). The methods are synchronous (return `result` from the set block), and the UI callers (RegionalImpactInbox.jsx:56/65, SettlementsPanel.jsx:988/996) consume only the synchronous return value and ignore the floated promise. So on rejection the promise is unhandled.

The rejection is reachable: in cloud mode (isConfigured) saves.update routes to supabaseUpdate (saves.js:195-215), which does `await supabase.from('settlements').update(...)` and `if (error) throw error` — it rejects on RLS denial, expired session, offline, or transient 5xx. saves.js:327 confirms the cloud routing.

Concrete consequence: before the persist fires, the set block has already (a) mutated state.savedSettlements[saveIdx].settlement with the condition, (b) updated active state.settlement/systemState, (c) marked the regionalGraph impact 'applied'/'resolved' via domainSetRegionalImpactStatus, and (d) called persistCampaignState -> persistCampaigns (96-104), which writes localStorage AND fires a .catch-guarded cloud sync of the campaign graph. The settlement mutation itself reaches the cloud ONLY through the unguarded persistSaveUpdate. So these are two independent cloud writes: the graph write is .catch'd and may succeed (marking the impact applied in the cloud campaign record) while the settlement write fails. Result: silent cloud divergence — graph says applied, but the settlement row's data/campaign_state never received the mutation — discoverable only on a later cloud reload. No rollback occurs, and the only signal is the unhandled rejection.

Two factors lower severity from the claimed level: (1) the rejection is NOT fully silent — a global window 'unhandledrejection' handler at errorReporter.js:86-88 forwards e.reason to reportError, so it is captured/reported (though the user still gets no actionable in-flow error and the data divergence is unrepaired); (2) this is an edge-case path (regional causality apply/resolve) that fires only when a cloud write fails mid-flow, not a main-path crash. The defect is real (a known-bad fire-and-forget-without-catch pattern, contradicted by the correct .catch usage in this same file at deletePersistedCampaign:127 and persistCampaigns:101) but its blast radius is a captured-but-unactioned rejection plus best-effort cloud divergence on a secondary feature path, so low rather than medium/high.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested direction is correct but the cleanest fix is to add a .catch at the two singular call sites (1108-1113 and 1172-1177), matching the established pattern in this file (deletePersistedCampaign:127, persistCampaigns:101): e.g. `persistSaveUpdate(result.saveId, {...})?.catch(() => {})` (the optional-chaining matters because persistSaveUpdate returns undefined when saveId/partial is falsy, line 155). Do NOT remove the re-throw from persistSaveUpdate itself — the re-throw is load-bearing for the awaited batch helper persistSaveUpdates (162-170, used at 965/991/1021), which relies on rejection propagating to await. Note the deeper, unfixed issue the report understates: even a proper .catch only suppresses the rejection; it does not resolve the two-write divergence (graph marked applied in cloud while the settlement mutation silently fails to persist). A more complete fix would surface a user-visible 'failed to save to cloud' signal and/or roll back the local graph status on settlement-write failure, but that is a larger design change beyond the scope of this defect.

confirmed.title: Library search/sort/filter UI is inert — filtered list is computed but never rendered
confirmed.evidence: P108/E-6 wires a LibraryToolbar (search + sort + filter chips) and computes `filteredSaves = applyLibraryFilters(saves, {query, sort, filters})` (lines 1023-1032), but `filteredSaves` is used ONLY for the toolbar's `visibleCount` prop (line 1072). The actual list renders campaign folders from `campaign.settlementIds.map(id => saves.find(...))` (line 1112) and the unassigned section from `unassignedSaves` which is derived from raw `saves` (line 1018, rendered line 1145). Typing a query or toggling chips updates the 'showing X of Y' count but never changes the cards shown. LibraryToolbar's own doc comment says its output 'is consumed by applyLibraryFilters(saves, state)' to filter the saves list, so this is drift, not intent.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Derive the rendered collections from `filteredSaves`: compute `unassignedSaves` from `filteredSaves`, and intersect each campaign's `settlementIds` resolution with the filtered set (or render a flat filtered list while a query/filter is active).

verdicts.reasoning:
Confirmed by reading the code. In src/components/SettlementsPanel.jsx, `filteredSaves` (the output of the search/sort/filter pipeline) is computed at lines 1026-1032 via `_applyLibraryFilters(saves, {query: libraryQuery, sort: librarySort, filters: libraryFilters})`. A targeted grep shows `filteredSaves` is referenced in exactly two places: its definition (1026) and `visibleCount={filteredSaves.length}` (line 1072). It never feeds any rendered collection.

The actually-rendered lists are derived from raw `saves`, not `filteredSaves`:
- Unassigned section: `unassignedSaves = saves.filter(s => !assignedIds.has(s.id))` (line 1018), rendered at lines 1145-1154. Derived from raw `saves`, only excluding campaign-assigned ids.
- Campaign folders: `campSaves = campaign.settlementIds.map(id => saves.find(s => s.id === id)).filter(Boolean)` (line 1112), rendered via CampaignFolder at line 1115. Resolved against raw `saves`, never intersected with the filtered set.

The state variables libraryQuery/librarySort/libraryFilters flow only into `filteredSaves` and the controlled LibraryToolbar props — nowhere else. Therefore typing a query, choosing a sort, or toggling a filter chip recomputes `filteredSaves` (changing the 'showing X of Y' count) but leaves the rendered cards and their order untouched. The sort is equally inert (applied at LibraryToolbar.jsx:84 inside applyLibraryFilters, which only feeds the count).

This contradicts explicit documented intent: applyLibraryFilters is a real, working query→filter→sort pipeline (LibraryToolbar.jsx:45-87), and the LibraryToolbar doc comment states its output is 'consumed by applyLibraryFilters(saves, state)' to filter the saves list (LibraryToolbar.jsx:9-12). So this is wiring drift, not intent.

Ruled out the common false-positive causes: the import is correctly aliased (`applyLibraryFilters as _applyLibraryFilters`, line 25) so it is not the underscore-import undefined-export bug — the function is real and works. The toolbar renders whenever `saves.length > 0` (line 1063), which is the normal main path for any user with saves, so it is not dead/unreachable. No upstream guard re-routes the data. There are no UI-integration tests pinning the wiring (grep for applyLibraryFilters/LibraryToolbar in the test tree returned nothing), so nothing enforces the correct behavior.

Severity high is appropriate: this is a shipped, user-facing feature (search/sort/filter across the settlement library) that is functionally broken on the main path — a broken feature per the severity rubric. It is not data loss/money/security, so not critical.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct in direction. Two clean options: (1) Render a flat filtered list from `filteredSaves` whenever any query/sort/filter is active, bypassing the folder grouping (simplest, matches 'visibleCount' semantics directly). (2) Keep grouping but derive both collections from the filtered set: compute `unassignedSaves` from `filteredSaves` instead of raw `saves`, and for each campaign resolve `campaign.settlementIds.map(id => filteredSaves.find(...)).filter(Boolean)`. Note one subtlety with option 2: the sort order from applyLibraryFilters (LibraryToolbar.jsx:84) is lost when you re-resolve campaign settlements by id in settlementIds order, and is partially lost for unassigned if you re-filter rather than reuse the sorted array — so to make the 'Sort' control meaningful you should preserve the filtered/sorted ordering when rendering (e.g., iterate filteredSaves and bucket into assigned/unassigned), or accept that sort only applies to a flat view (option 1). A flat filtered list while a filter is active (option 1) is the most faithful to the toolbar's documented contract and avoids the ordering-loss caveat.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED via end-to-end read of both files. In src/components/SettlementsPanel.jsx, `filteredSaves` (line 1026) is computed by `_applyLibraryFilters(saves, {query, sort, filters})` but is referenced exactly ONCE — at line 1072 as `visibleCount={filteredSaves.length}`. grep confirms no other consumer and no child component receives it. The actual cards render from two raw-`saves`-derived sources that never touch the filter: (1) campaign folders use `campSaves = campaign.settlementIds.map(id => saves.find(s => s.id === id)).filter(Boolean)` (lines 1111-1112), and (2) the unassigned section uses `unassignedSaves = saves.filter(s => !assignedIds.has(s.id))` (line 1018, rendered line 1145). CampaignFolder (def line 227) does no filtering of its own — it renders whatever `settlements` prop it gets (lines 279/324).

Concrete failure path: a user with >=1 save lands on the Settlements tab; LibraryToolbar renders (line 1063, gated ONLY by `saves.length > 0`). User types a query -> `libraryQuery` updates -> `filteredSaves` shrinks -> the toolbar count label (LibraryToolbar.jsx lines 205-207) flips from "17 settlements" to e.g. "2 of 17". But every card stays on screen because the lists read raw `saves`. Result: search box, sort dropdown, and the canon/draft/has-neighbours/has-pending-edits filter chips are all inert, and the "X of Y" label actively contradicts the visible cards.

This is drift, not intent: LibraryToolbar.jsx's own header doc (lines 9-12) states "Output is consumed by `applyLibraryFilters(saves, state)`" to filter the saves list, and `applyLibraryFilters` (lines 45-87) is a fully-functional query+filter+sort pipeline with 11 contract tests (tests/components/libraryToolbar.test.js) and a docs entry (docs/critique-implementation-status.md line 46) that advertises working "Search across name/tier/NPCs/factions; sort by recent/name/tier; filter chips." The function works; the wiring to the rendered list was never completed.

Severity = medium: a shipped, always-on UI feature (no flag gates it — the `librarySearch` flag named in the docs does not exist anywhere in src/, only the doc references it) is completely non-functional on a main user path, and the count label misleads. It is not data-loss/money/security and does not corrupt simulation output, so not high/critical; but it is a broken feature visible to every user with saved settlements, not a mere edge case.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested mental fix ("render filteredSaves instead of saves") is INCOMPLETE because the list is split into campaign folders + an unassigned section, and the filter must propagate into BOTH, not just the unassigned set. Correct approach: build a fast-lookup set of allowed ids from filteredSaves (e.g. `const visibleIds = new Set(filteredSaves.map(s => s.id))`), then (a) for each campaign, filter campSaves to `campSaves.filter(s => visibleIds.has(s.id))` so campaign-assigned settlements also respond to search/chips, and (b) replace the unassigned render source so it lists only filtered-and-unassigned saves (e.g. `filteredSaves.filter(s => !assignedIds.has(s.id))`). Note `applyLibraryFilters` also SORTS; if sort order is meant to apply within campaign folders you must preserve filteredSaves' order rather than using a Set (which loses order) — derive each folder's list by intersecting in filteredSaves order. Also consider: when a query is active and a campaign folder ends up empty, decide whether to hide the folder (currently CampaignFolder renders an empty state) and whether to suppress the per-folder/unassigned count mismatches. Finally, the docs claim this is behind a `librarySearch` flag, but no such flag exists in src/ — either wire the flag or fix the docs; right now the broken UI ships unconditionally.

confirmed.title: Credit balance is fetched only once at app mount — never refreshed on in-session sign-in, so AI actions are blocked and the purchase modal is pushed despite server-side credits
confirmed.evidence: `fetchCreditBalance().then(bal => setCreditBalance(bal))` runs once in App's mount effect (App.jsx:127-154) and is the ONLY call site in the app (grep confirms). For an anonymous visitor it returns 0 (stripe.js:168-172 returns 0 when no user). authSlice's onAuthChange SIGNED_IN handler (authSlice.js:157-216) sets auth fields, replays auth intents, and sends welcome email — but never refetches credits. So after the primary funnel path (anon generates → signs up in the AuthModal without a page reload), `creditBalance` stays 0. aiSlice.requestNarrative/requestDailyLife client-gate on this stale value: `if (!elevated && creditBalance < cost) { aiError = 'Insufficient credits…'; setPurchaseModalOpen(true); }` (aiSlice.js:279-283, 450, 585) — telling a user with real ledger credits (e.g. the welcome grant) that they have none and pushing them to buy more, until they hard-reload. AuthModal also displays the stale `creditBalance` (AuthModal.jsx:126).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Refresh the balance on auth transitions: in authSlice's SIGNED_IN handler (or an App effect keyed on authUserId), call fetchCreditBalance() and setCreditBalance(); clear it on SIGNED_OUT. Alternatively drop the client-side pre-check and let the edge function's server-side ledger check be authoritative.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED, with one caveat about the headline example.

Verified the mechanism end to end:
- src/App.jsx:152 `fetchCreditBalance().then(bal => setCreditBalance(bal))` runs inside the mount effect whose deps are `[initAuth, initOnboarding, setCreditBalance]` (App.jsx:154) — none change on sign-in, so it fires exactly once per page load. grep confirms it is the only non-post-spend call site of fetchCreditBalance (the other writes to creditBalance at aiSlice.js:380/508/660 and WizardNewsPanel.jsx:250 all require a SUCCESSFUL AI call, i.e. they only run AFTER the client gate has already been passed).
- For an anonymous session, fetchCreditBalance returns 0 (stripe.js:168-172: no user => return 0).
- In-session sign-in is real and reload-free: AuthModal renders `<AuthPanel initialMode="signin" onAuthed={onClose} />` (AuthModal.jsx:83); a successful sign-in just closes the modal. The SIGNED_IN handler in authSlice.js:157-219 rebuilds `state.auth`, replays auth intents, and sends the welcome email, but never calls fetchCreditBalance/setCreditBalance. clearAuth on SIGNED_OUT likewise does not reset/refetch.
- The client pre-flight gate reads the stale value and pushes the purchase modal: aiSlice.js:279-283 (requestNarrative), 450-451 (requestDailyLife), 585-586 (progression): `if (!elevated && creditBalance < cost) { aiError='Insufficient credits...'; setPurchaseModalOpen(true); return; }`. AccountPage.jsx:417/420 and AuthModal.jsx:126 also display the stale balance.

So a user who browses anonymously and signs in via the in-session AuthModal sees creditBalance stuck at 0 — and is told they have no credits and pushed to buy — until a hard reload, even though the server ledger holds their real balance. This is a genuine broken-feature defect on a monetized path.

Caveat / partial refutation of the title's framing: the title and EVIDENCE lean on the "welcome grant" case (brand-new free signup). That specific example is weak. The welcome grant is 1 credit (migration 017:60 `values (new.id, 1, 'welcome')`), but the cheapest AI feature costs 2 (FAST_AI_COSTS narrative) or 3 (NEW_AI_COSTS narrative) per config/pricing.js:64-74. A brand-new free user therefore genuinely cannot afford any AI action, and the server-side spend_credits RPC would also reject them (generate-narrative/index.ts:2006-2024) — so blocking them is correct, not a false block. The real, unambiguous harm case is a RETURNING user with a real positive balance (Cartographer: 30 monthly credits per TIERS.cartographer.monthlyCredits, or anyone who bought a credit pack) who was anonymous then signs in in-session: they are falsely told they have 0 and pushed to repurchase until reload.

Note on impact bound: this is not a money/data/security bug — the edge function's spend_credits RPC is server-authoritative (no over/under-charge), so the worst outcome is a blocked legitimate action plus an unnecessary purchase prompt. That still qualifies as high (broken feature on a paying-user path, surviving until manual reload).
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct in direction. Refetch on auth transition is the right move. Concretely: in authSlice's SIGNED_IN/TOKEN_REFRESHED handler (authSlice.js:160-219), after setting state.auth, lazy-import stripe.js and call fetchCreditBalance().then(bal => set(state => { state.creditBalance = bal; })); on SIGNED_OUT (clearAuth) reset creditBalance to 0. An App effect keyed on authUserId would also work and is simpler to reason about (add fetchCreditBalance there with authUserId in the dep array), but note the existing mount effect imports stripe.js lazily, so keep that pattern. The alternate fix (drop the client pre-check, rely on the server spend_credits gate) is cleaner long-term and removes the whole stale-gate class, but you'd need to keep surfacing the server's `Insufficient credits. Need N, have M.` error and trigger the purchase modal from that server response instead of the client pre-check. Either way, when adding a refetch, gate it on a real user (fetchCreditBalance already returns 0 for anon) and avoid resetting a freshly-known post-spend balance.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED. The credit balance is fetched exactly once at App mount and never refreshed on in-session sign-in, so the client-side AI gates block users who have real server-side credits.

Trace, end to end:
1. `creditBalance` initializes to 0 (src/store/creditsSlice.js:35) and is NOT persisted — store/index.js partialize (lines 63-74) persists only config + four toggle maps, never creditBalance. So on every fresh page load it starts at 0.
2. The ONLY fetch site is the App mount effect: `fetchCreditBalance().then(bal => setCreditBalance(bal))` (src/App.jsx:152), inside a useEffect whose deps are `[initAuth, initOnboarding, setCreditBalance]` (line 154) — all stable references — so it runs once and never re-runs on auth change. grep confirms App.jsx:152 is the sole fetchCreditBalance call site (the other setCreditBalance at WizardNewsPanel.jsx:250 and aiSlice.js:380/508/660 only sync from a *successful* AI call's `creditsRemaining`, which hasn't happened yet on the first action).
3. For an anonymous visitor, fetchCreditBalance returns 0 (src/lib/stripe.js:171-172: no user => return 0).
4. The user signs in/up in-session via the AuthModal — AuthModal.jsx:83 renders `<AuthPanel initialMode="signin" onAuthed={onClose}/>`; AuthPanel.handleSignIn (auth/AuthPanel.jsx:94-106) calls `authSignIn` then closes the modal. No page reload.
5. authSlice's onAuthChange SIGNED_IN handler (src/store/authSlice.js:160-219) sets auth fields, sends the welcome email, fires analytics, and replays authIntents — but NEVER refetches credits. clearAuth (SIGNED_OUT, lines 91-102) likewise never resets creditBalance, so a prior user's balance also lingers after sign-out (secondary facet of the same root cause).
6. Result: creditBalance stays at the stale fresh-load value of 0. The first AI action gates on it: requestNarrative `if (!elevated && creditBalance < cost) { aiError='Insufficient credits (need X, have 0)'; setPurchaseModalOpen(true); return; }` (aiSlice.js:279-283), and identically requestDailyLife (450-454) and progression (585-589). AuthModal.jsx:126 also displays the stale balance.

Concrete failure path with real consequence: a RETURNING user who previously bought a credit pack (e.g. credits_25) signs in via the AuthModal mid-session. Their server ledger has 25 credits, but the client has 0, so the very next narrative/daily-life click is falsely blocked with "Insufficient credits … have 0" and the purchase modal is force-opened — pushing a paying customer to buy credits they already own, until they hard-reload. The welcome-credit grant is real (DB trigger on_auth_user_created grants 1 credit per new account, supabase/migrations/017_fix_credit_auth_integrity.sql:50-66), though note the cheapest narrative costs 2 credits (pricing.js:71-72), so a brand-new 1-credit account would coincidentally be blocked correctly anyway — the unambiguous false-block case is the returning/topped-up user. Importantly, this is NOT a money-loss or over-spend bug: the server (spend_credits RPC + generate-narrative edge function cost gate) is authoritative and intact, so no credits are ever wrongly deducted; the harm is a broken monetized feature / false upsell on the primary funnel path, recoverable by a page reload.

Severity high (broken feature + wrong client state on a paid/funnel path, false purchase-modal push), not critical (no data loss, no money loss, no crash, server ledger authoritative, recoverable via reload).
verdicts.fix_note: Refetch the balance whenever auth resolves, and reset it on sign-out. Cleanest fix: in authSlice onAuthChange (authSlice.js), on SIGNED_IN / TOKEN_REFRESHED lazy-import stripe.js and call `fetchCreditBalance().then(bal => get().setCreditBalance(bal))` (lazy import keeps stripe.js out of the auth-critical bundle, matching the existing emailLifecycle/analytics lazy-import pattern in that same handler). Also reset `creditBalance` to 0 inside clearAuth (authSlice.js:91-102) so a prior user's balance doesn't leak after sign-out. Alternatively/additionally, add an App.jsx effect keyed on authUserId/authTier that refetches credits (parallel to the existing loadCampaigns effect at App.jsx:156-160) — but the authSlice approach is more robust because it also covers TOKEN_REFRESHED and the cross-tab sign-in case. Note the gate at aiSlice.js:279/450/585 is correct to keep as a UX pre-flight; this fix just makes the value it reads accurate. No change needed to the server gate — it is already authoritative.

confirmed.title: Canonical save path skips neighbour-network migration and bidirectional linking — the only code that did it (saveCurrentSettlement) is now dead
confirmed.evidence: SettlementsPanel.saveCurrentSettlement (lines 798-890) performs three save-time tasks: migrate generator `neighborRelationship` → `neighbourNetwork` (829-835), bidirectionally link the partner save with canonical edge roles (841-881), and generate inter-settlement NPC pairs + cross-settlement conflicts (872-873). But its UI trigger was removed (comment at 1076-1079: 'saving a fresh draft lives in the generate flow (SaveToLibraryButton)'), making it, `isDuplicate` (795), and the `saved` state (612) dead code — nothing in the file references them. The canonical save path, GenerateWizard's SaveToLibraryButton (GenerateWizard.jsx:215-220) and the SAVE_SETTLEMENT auth intent (GenerateWizard.jsx:242-248), calls `savesService.save()` directly, which only runs migrateSaveToV2 (campaignState defaults) — lib/saves.js has no neighborRelationship→neighbourNetwork migration and never calls buildInterSettlementNPCs/generateCrossSettlementConflicts (grep: those are only invoked from the dead path and SettlementDetail's manual Link flow). domain/normalizeSettlement.js contains no neighbourNetwork handling either. Result: every new save with a generation-time neighbour persists the raw `neighborRelationship` only — partner saves get no back-link, and NetworkEffectsPanel/regionalGraph (which read `neighbourNetwork`, regionalGraph.js:220) silently miss these relationships.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Move the neighborRelationship→neighbourNetwork migration + bidirectional partner linking into lib/saves.js save() (or a shared domain helper invoked by both SaveToLibraryButton and the auth-intent), then delete the dead saveCurrentSettlement/isDuplicate/saved code.

verdicts.reasoning:
Every premise of the claim is verified against the actual code.

DEAD CODE: SettlementsPanel.saveCurrentSettlement (798-890), isDuplicate (795), and the `saved` state (612) are referenced nowhere else in the file (grep returns only lines 795 and 798; `setSaved` is called only inside the dead function). The UI trigger was removed per the comment at 1076-1079 ("saving a fresh draft lives in the generate flow (SaveToLibraryButton)").

GENERATOR EMITS ONLY THE SCALAR: generators/steps/assembleSettlement.js:59 emits `neighborRelationship` (a scalar object). No generator anywhere emits the `neighbourNetwork` array (grep across src/generators, normalizeSettlement.js, settlement.schema.js returns nothing).

CANONICAL SAVE PATH DOES NO MIGRATION: SaveToLibraryButton.handleSave (GenerateWizard.jsx:215-220) and the SAVE_SETTLEMENT auth-intent (store/index.js:102-110) both call savesService.save() directly. lib/saves.js (supabaseSave 167-193, migrateSaveToV2 91-109, migrateSettlementShape -> normalizeSettlement) contains zero neighborRelationship->neighbourNetwork conversion (normalizeSettlement.js:1-174 has no neighbour handling at all). saves.js:180 writes `neighbour_links: v2.settlement?.neighbourNetwork || null`, so a fresh save persists neighbour_links = null. The read path (supabaseList 128-164) selects neighbour_links but never maps it back into settlement.neighbourNetwork — no self-healing on reload.

CONSUMERS READ THE ARRAY, NOT THE SCALAR: regionalGraph.js:220 (NetworkEffects/regional graph), roadNetwork.js:79, map/RelationshipEdges.jsx:69-70 (world-map edges), generateCampaignPDF.js:274/311/627, and settlement/EventComposer.jsx:117/227 all read neighbourNetwork with no scalar fallback. So a fresh save carrying only the scalar is invisible to all of those.

The code's own comment confirms the intended-but-now-broken contract: RelationshipsTab.jsx:53 — "Use unified neighbourNetwork array (generator's neighborRelationship is migrated to this at save time)". That migration is exactly what was lost when the only code doing it went dead. Additionally, bidirectional partner back-linking plus buildInterSettlementNPCs/generateCrossSettlementConflicts run only from the dead path (872-873) and the manual Link flow (932-933), so generation-time neighbours never produce a partner back-link or cross-settlement NPCs/conflicts on the canonical path.

Mitigating (reduces blast radius, not existence): the open dossier (OutputContainer.jsx:355) and RelationshipsTab (55-68) fall back to the scalar, so the detail view still shows the generated neighbour. Only settlements generated with a neighbour import are affected.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct in direction (move migration + bidirectional linking into a shared domain helper invoked by both SaveToLibraryButton and the SAVE_SETTLEMENT intent, then delete the dead saveCurrentSettlement/isDuplicate/saved). One refinement: the bidirectional partner back-link requires UPDATING the partner save's row too, but the canonical entrypoint uses savesService.save() (a single insert), which cannot touch the partner. The shared helper must perform a batch mutation (insert of the new save + update of the partner), i.e. route through savesService.mutateBatch rather than save(), or the partner back-link still won't persist. The scalar->neighbourNetwork migration alone (no partner present) can be done inline in save(), but the full feature parity needs the batch path. Also note saves.js writes neighbour_links from settlement.neighbourNetwork on both insert (180) and update (201), so once the embedded settlement carries the array the column will populate correctly.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED at every layer with read-only reproduction against the real modules.

DEAD CODE: SettlementsPanel.saveCurrentSettlement (src/components/SettlementsPanel.jsx:798-890), isDuplicate (795), and saved/setSaved (612, 884) have no live references anywhere — the UI trigger was deleted (comment at 1076-1079). Grep for callers returned empty. This was the ONLY code that (a) migrated generator neighborRelationship -> neighbourNetwork (829-835), (b) bidirectionally back-linked the partner save (841-881), and (c) generated inter-settlement NPCs + cross-settlement conflicts (872-873 via buildInterSettlementNPCs / generateCrossSettlementConflicts).

CANONICAL PATH MISSES IT: The live save path is GenerateWizard SaveToLibraryButton (GenerateWizard.jsx:215) and the SAVE_SETTLEMENT auth intent (242-248), both calling savesService.save() directly. lib/saves.js only ever copies an already-present neighbourNetwork (lines 59, 180, 201) — never derives it. migrateSaveToV2 (saves.js:91) only injects campaignState. normalizeSettlement.js and settlementMigrations.js contain no neighbour handling (grep confirmed). The generator DOES emit neighborRelationship (assembleSettlement.js:59).

REPRODUCED (node one-liners against real modules):
1. normalizeSettlement(generated-with-neighborRelationship) -> neighbourNetwork absent, neighborRelationship retained.
2. deriveRegionalGraph(...) -> 0 links / 1 node. regionalGraph.js:218-221 builds sources from neighbours/neighbourNetwork/neighborNetwork, never the singular neighborRelationship.
3. deriveRegionalGraphFromSaves([Thornhold(rival of Greyford via neighborRelationship), Greyford]) -> 2 nodes, 0 edges. neighbourLinksFor (domain/region/graph.js:261-267) reads ONLY neighbourNetwork/neighborNetwork/neighbourLinks. This is the production campaign graph builder (campaignSlice.js:583).

CONSEQUENCE: Every new save whose settlement was generated with a neighbour persists raw neighborRelationship only. The partner save gets no reciprocal back-link; the campaign regional graph produces no edge; map RelationshipEdges, mapProfile.js:110, and aiGrounding.js:329 (AI prose grounding) all silently miss the relationship; no inter-settlement NPCs / cross-settlement conflicts are generated. This is wrong simulation/regional output and silent data loss for the entire network feature — high, not critical (no crash, no money/auth; and RelationshipsTab.jsx:55-68 still synthesizes a display-only _liveEntry from neighborRelationship so the neighbour is not 100% invisible on-screen).
verdicts.fix_note:
The evidence's framing is accurate, but the suggested remediation needs scoping because the three lost tasks have different homes:

1. neighborRelationship -> neighbourNetwork migration is self-contained (needs only the settlement itself) and SHOULD move into the save boundary so it always runs: best placed in normalizeSettlement.js (so it runs on read AND via migrateSettlementShape in saves.list) OR inside savesService.save/migrateSaveToV2. Putting it in normalizeSettlement is cleanest because deriveRegionalGraph/neighbourLinksFor and RelationshipsTab all already assume neighbourNetwork is the unified field, and normalize runs at every save/load/PDF/AI boundary.

2. Bidirectional partner back-link and inter-settlement NPC/conflict generation CANNOT be done inside savesService.save() as-is: they require the full saves list (to findSaveByName the partner) and a second write to the partner row. savesService.save() only inserts one row and has no library context. So this part must live either in a component/store action that has the saves array (e.g. the post-save flow after GenerateWizard's save resolves, mirroring SettlementDetail's manual Link flow at SettlementsPanel.jsx:932-933), or in a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that takes the new save + resolves/links the partner server-side. Simply deleting the dead saveCurrentSettlement without porting the linking logic would lock in the regression.

3. Lowest-effort partial fix that restores the regional graph without the back-link: add a neighborRelationship fallback to neighbourLinksFor (graph.js:261) and to deriveRegionalGraph sources (regionalGraph.js:218-221). That makes the singular field visible to graphs/maps even unmigrated, but still won't create the partner back-link, inter-settlement NPCs, or cross-settlement conflicts — so it is a mitigation, not a complete fix.

Also: when removing the dead code, drop isDuplicate (795) and saved/setSaved (612, 884) together, and the now-unused buildInterSettlementNPCs/generateCrossSettlementConflicts imports if the Detail Link flow is the only remaining consumer (verify — SettlementsPanel.jsx:932-933 still uses them, so keep them).

confirmed.title: WizardNextSteps compares auth tier against 'wanderer' (a pricing key, not an auth tier) — anonymous users get the wrong save guidance
confirmed.evidence: `const signedIn = !!authTier && authTier !== 'wanderer';` — but `auth.tier` is documented and implemented as 'anon' | 'free' | 'premium' (store/authSlice.js:64); 'wanderer' is the marketing key in config/pricing.js. So `signedIn` is true for anonymous users ('anon' !== 'wanderer'). In saveStep() (lines 29-50), an anon who can't save then falls into the signed-in branch and is told "Save it. Free up a slot or upgrade — You've reached your library's save cap" instead of "Save it. Create a free account — Free accounts keep your settlements and unlock Town+ tiers". This card renders after every generation for anonymous users (WizardNextSteps is mounted unconditionally from GenerateWizard.jsx:936), i.e. wrong copy on the main signup-funnel surface.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change the check to `authTier !== 'anon'` (or `useStore(s => s.auth.tier !== 'anon')`).

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified against the actual code. WizardNextSteps.jsx:99 computes `const signedIn = !!authTier && authTier !== 'wanderer';` where authTier = useStore(s => s.auth?.tier). authSlice.js confirms auth.tier is one of 'anon' | 'free' | 'premium' (state default 'anon' at :64; TIER_GATE keys at :34-38; resolveTier at :54-56 only ever yields 'premium' or tier||'free'). A grep shows 'wanderer' exists ONLY as a pricing config key (config/pricing.js:168-169, 244), PricingPage.jsx, and copy files — never as an auth.tier value. Therefore for an anonymous user auth.tier==='anon', and 'anon' !== 'wanderer' is true, so signedIn is wrongly true.

canSave() (authSlice.js:339-343) returns TIER_GATE['anon'].maxSaves > 0 → 0 > 0 → false for anon. So saveStep({ canSave:false, signedIn:true }) (WizardNextSteps.jsx:29-50) skips the canSave branch, takes the signedIn branch, and returns label "Save it. Free up a slot or upgrade" / hint "You've reached your library's save cap." The correct anon copy in the final return (label "Save it. Create a free account" / hint "Free accounts keep your settlements and unlock Town+ tiers") is unreachable for anonymous users.

WizardNextSteps is mounted unconditionally at GenerateWizard.jsx:936 (component self-gates only on `if (!settlement) return null`), so this wrong copy renders on the main post-generation surface for every anonymous user — the primary signup-funnel moment.

I attempted to refute via the usual false-positive causes: (a) dead/unreachable path — no, it renders unconditionally post-generation; (b) upstream guard — none, signedIn is computed inline; (c) intentional per comments — the JSDoc at line 59 even says "non-wanderer", revealing the author conflated the pricing key with the auth tier, confirming the mistake rather than excusing it; (d) API differs — auth.tier genuinely never equals 'wanderer'. The claim holds.

Severity: medium is fair but at the low end of medium — it is incorrect copy/UX on a funnel surface with no functional state corruption, data loss, money, or crash. The suggested fix (authTier !== 'anon') is correct.
verdicts.fix_note: Suggested fix `authTier !== 'anon'` is correct. To preserve the existing null-guard cleanly, prefer `const signedIn = !!authTier && authTier !== 'anon';` (functionally authTier is always one of the three valid strings, so the bare `authTier !== 'anon'` also works). Also worth updating the now-misleading JSDoc at WizardNextSteps.jsx:59 which says "non-wanderer" — that comment is what encoded the original confusion.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED with full end-to-end trace. The claim is accurate in every load-bearing detail.

Possible values of auth.tier are exactly {'anon','free','premium'}: authSlice.js:64 defaults to 'anon', resetAuth (line 93) sets 'anon', resolveTier (lines 54-56) returns 'premium' for elevated roles else the tier or 'free', and the upstream source auth.js normalizeTier (lines 32-34) only ever returns 'premium' | 'free' | fallback 'free'. The string 'wanderer' is purely a pricing/marketing key (pricing.js:168-169 TIERS.wanderer; LABELS at 244). grep confirms 'wanderer' never appears as an auth.tier value anywhere in src.

Concrete failure path for an anonymous user (the main funnel target — anon ceiling is 'town', TIER_GATE line 35, so anon generation is a fully supported primary path):
1. Anon generates a settlement; WizardNextSteps mounts unconditionally inside the settlement-display branch (GenerateWizard.jsx:936, no auth/flag gate; component returns null only when !settlement, line 97).
2. authTier = 'anon'.
3. canSave() → TIER_GATE['anon'].maxSaves is 0; 0 > 0 is false → canSave = false (authSlice.js:342).
4. WizardNextSteps.jsx:99 computes signedIn = !!'anon' && 'anon' !== 'wanderer' = true && true = true. This is the bug: the comparison should be against 'anon', not the pricing key 'wanderer'.
5. saveStep({canSave:false, signedIn:true}) skips the canSave branch and enters the signedIn branch (lines 37-44), returning label 'Save it. Free up a slot or upgrade' / hint "You've reached your library's save cap."
6. The anon user — who has no account and no library — is shown account-holder cap/upgrade copy instead of the intended conversion CTA 'Save it. Create a free account' / 'Free accounts keep your settlements and unlock Town+ tiers.' (lines 45-49).

The pure builder buildNextSteps/saveStep is correct and well-tested (tests/components/wizardNextSteps.test.js: line 33-36 asserts anonymous → 'free account' framing for signedIn:false). The tests pass because they feed signedIn directly to the pure builder, bypassing the buggy line-99 wiring; the component-level signedIn derivation has no test coverage, which is why this slipped through. The author's intent (doc comment line 59: 'whether the user is authenticated (non-wanderer)' and the test contract) is unambiguously that anon → signedIn=false.

Consequence: wrong, nonsensical copy on the primary signup-funnel surface (post-generation, every anonymous visitor), and the actual create-a-free-account conversion message is never shown to the audience it was written for. This is a real conversion/UX defect on a money-adjacent funnel, but it is copy-only — no data loss, no security, no determinism or simulation-output impact, no crash — so medium, not high. Not critical because the canonical Save control and its own gating live elsewhere (GenerateWizard.jsx:925); only this guidance card's hint text is wrong.
verdicts.fix_note: Fix is correct: change WizardNextSteps.jsx:99 from `authTier !== 'wanderer'` to `authTier !== 'anon'`, i.e. `const signedIn = !!authTier && authTier !== 'anon';`. This yields signedIn=false for 'anon' (correct → free-account framing) and signedIn=true for 'free'/'premium' (correct → library/cap framing), matching both the doc comment intent and the existing pure-builder tests. Since authTier is undefined while auth is still loading (or absent), the `!!authTier` guard already short-circuits to false there, which also maps to the anonymous framing — acceptable. Optional hardening (not required): also add a unit/integration test that exercises the component-level signedIn derivation (e.g. render with auth.tier='anon' and assert the 'free account' label), since the current tests only cover the pure builder and would not have caught this.

confirmed.title: OutputContainer crashes on null settlement: `settlement.stress` is evaluated before the deliberately deferred null check
confirmed.evidence: The component documents a hooks-safe design: 'do not early-return here… We instead defer the null check until after all hooks have been called' (lines 207-212), with `earlyExitOnNoSettlement` returned at line 567. But line 445-447 run unconditionally in the body BEFORE that return: `const settlement = rawSettlement; const stressObj = settlement.stress ? … : null;` — no optional chaining. With `rawSettlement` null (no prop, empty store), this throws TypeError before reaching the guard, so the early-exit path can never work. Current callers happen to guard (GenerateWizard renders it only when `settlement` is truthy; SettlementDetail/PublicDossierView pass a checked prop), so this is latent — but it defeats the explicit defensive design and will crash on any future direct render without a settlement.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Use `rawSettlement?.stress` (and audit lines 445-447 for other unguarded accesses), or move the lines 445-447 derivation below the line 567 early return.

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified every code fact in the claim against the actual source.

CODE FACTS (all confirmed):
- src/components/OutputContainer.jsx:174 — `const rawSettlement = propSettlement || storeSettlement;` so rawSettlement can be null/undefined.
- Lines 207-212 — explicit comment documenting a deliberate hooks-safe design: do NOT early-return; defer the null check until after all hooks. `const earlyExitOnNoSettlement = !rawSettlement;` at line 212.
- Lines 445-447 — run unconditionally in the component body: `const settlement = rawSettlement; const stressObj = settlement.stress ? (Array.isArray(settlement.stress) ? settlement.stress[0] : settlement.stress) : null;` — NO optional chaining. With rawSettlement === null, `settlement.stress` throws TypeError before reaching the guard.
- Line 567 — `if (earlyExitOnNoSettlement) return null;` is the deferred guard, which sits AFTER the crashing access at 446. I confirmed there is no earlier body-level early return between the hooks and 567 (the returns at 416-439 and 457-562 are inside the renderTab/renderNarrativeButtons helper closures, not the body).
- Corroborating inconsistency: line 355 in the same body region uses `rawSettlement?.neighborRelationship` (optional chaining), showing the surrounding code is meant to be null-safe — so line 446's bare access is a genuine oversight, not an intentional invariant.

So the claim is accurate: line 446 evaluates settlement.stress before the deliberately-deferred null check, which would crash on a null settlement and renders the earlyExitOnNoSettlement machinery (212/567) effectively dead — it can never fire without crashing first. This is a real contradiction between the documented defensive design and the code.

REACHABILITY (refutation attempt — the only axis that could downgrade this): All three current callers guard against null, so the crash cannot manifest today:
- GenerateWizard.jsx:917 `<OutputContainer hideHeader />` is wrapped at line 849 in `{settlement && showOutput && !pipelineRevealActive && (...)}`; storeSettlement is truthy there.
- SettlementDetail.jsx:849 is wrapped at line 843 in `{detail.settlement && ...}`.
- PublicDossierView.jsx:118 has an early-return guard at line 41 `if (!dossier || !dossier.settlement) return ...` ensuring the passed settlement is truthy.

Therefore the defect is genuinely LATENT — not reachable from any live path. The reviewer explicitly and correctly acknowledges this ("Current callers happen to guard... so this is latent"). I could not refute the core claim; the code-level evidence holds exactly as described. It is a real defect (the deferred-null-check seam is broken / dead), just not currently triggerable.
verdicts.fix_note: Suggested fix is correct. Cleanest is `const stressObj = settlement?.stress ? (Array.isArray(settlement.stress) ? settlement.stress[0] : settlement.stress) : null;` (the `const settlement = rawSettlement` aliasing is harmless; the unsafe part is the `.stress` access). Alternatively move lines 445-447 below the line 567 early return. While there, the rest of the body between hooks and 567 should be quick-audited, but note line 355 already uses optional chaining, so 446 appears to be the lone offender. Severity is medium-as-claimed if you weight defeating an explicit safety mechanism, but I rate it low because no live caller can reach the null path (all three guard upstream) — it cannot crash main flow today; it only crashes a hypothetical future direct render with no prop and empty store.

verdicts.reasoning:
CONFIRMED as a real latent defect, exactly as described. Traced end-to-end against the actual code.

Code facts (all verified at the cited lines in src/components/OutputContainer.jsx):
- Lines 207-212: documented hooks-safe design — "do not early-return here ... We instead defer the null check until after all hooks have been called."
- Line 212: `const earlyExitOnNoSettlement = !rawSettlement;`
- Line 174: `const rawSettlement = propSettlement || storeSettlement;` (can be null/undefined when neither prop nor store has a settlement).
- Lines 445-447: `const settlement = rawSettlement; const stressObj = settlement.stress ? (Array.isArray(...) ? settlement.stress[0] : settlement.stress) : null;` — raw `.stress` access, NO optional chaining, executed unconditionally in the component body.
- Line 567: the deferred guard `if (earlyExitOnNoSettlement) return null;` runs AFTER line 446.

Failure trace: If rawSettlement is null at render, the function body reaches line 446 and evaluates `settlement.stress`, throwing `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'stress')` BEFORE line 567 can early-exit. This defeats the explicitly documented defensive contract — the early-exit path is unreachable for a null settlement.

I verified line 446 is the FIRST unconditional settlement dereference in the body: earlier code is null-safe (line 297 hasCompass uses `!!(o && ...)`; line 318 compassSource is null-safe; line 325 uses `rawSettlement || {}`; line 355 uses `rawSettlement?.`). The functions renderTab (deref `activeSettlement`/s) and renderNarrativeButtons are defined before the guard but only INVOKED after it (renderTab at line 867, renderNarrativeButtons at 615/642), so they never run on a null settlement. stressObj itself is only consumed at line 600, which is after the guard.

Severity = low (not higher) because it is genuinely latent — there is no currently-reachable crash. All three call sites guard, which I independently verified:
- GenerateWizard.jsx:849 gates the block with `{settlement && showOutput && !pipelineRevealActive && (` and the `<OutputContainer hideHeader />` at 917 reads storeSettlement (== the gated `settlement`).
- SettlementDetail.jsx:843 gates with `{detail.settlement && ...}` and passes `settlement={detail.settlement}`.
- PublicDossierView.jsx:41 early-returns on `!dossier || !dossier.settlement` before reaching the render at line 118.
No test, route, or Storybook entry renders OutputContainer without a settlement. So this is a real-but-dormant contract violation: it breaks the stated hooks-safe defensive design and will crash on any future direct render without a settlement, but harms no user flow today.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct in spirit. Cleanest fix: MOVE lines 445-447 (the `settlement`/`stressObj` computation) to below the guard at line 567, since stressObj is only consumed at line 600 (after the guard) and `const settlement = rawSettlement` is used only at lines 584-600 (all post-guard). This restores the documented design exactly: no settlement deref happens until the null check has passed. Adding optional chaining (`settlement?.stress`) at line 446 also prevents the throw but is inferior — it papers over the contract violation while leaving `const settlement = rawSettlement` and the line-584+ header derefs as the real guard-protected consumers; relocation is the change that actually honors the deferred-null-check intent. Note `stressObj` is purely cosmetic (a header chip label at line 600), so even an unguarded null today would only ever crash an empty header render, never corrupt data or billing.

confirmed.title: 'Reroll History' is unreachable: its only UI surface (the dossier header reroll button) never renders on any live path
confirmed.evidence: REROLLABLE = { npcs: 'Reroll NPCs', history: 'Reroll History' } (line 110) renders only inside the header block (line 580-616), gated by `REROLLABLE[selectedTab] && onRegenerate`. But: (1) the live editor (GenerateWizard.jsx:917) passes `hideHeader`, so the whole header — including this button — is suppressed; (2) SettlementDetail (SettlementDetail.jsx:849) and PublicDossierView (PublicDossierView.jsx:118) pass `readOnly`, which nulls `onRegenerate` (line 188). NPCs keep a working in-tab reroll because renderTab passes `onRerollNPCs` to NPCsTab (line 424, NPCsTab.jsx:68), but `case 'history'` (line 425) passes no reroll prop and HistoryTab has none. So the store's regenSection('history') capability is dead UI everywhere.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Pass an `onReroll` prop to HistoryTab from renderTab (mirroring NPCsTab) or render the reroll affordance outside the hideHeader-gated header.

verdicts.reasoning:
The claim is accurate on every load-bearing point, verified against the actual code.

1. REROLLABLE includes both npcs and history (OutputContainer.jsx:110). The history reroll button is rendered only at lines 603-606, gated by `REROLLABLE[selectedTab] && onRegenerate`, and that whole block sits inside the header which is rendered as `hideHeader ? null : ...` (line 580).

2. onRegenerate = readOnly ? null : storeRegenerate (line 188), and storeRegenerate = s.regenSection (line 143).

3. There are exactly three live JSX render sites of OutputContainer in the entire app (confirmed via grep `<OutputContainer`):
   - GenerateWizard.jsx:917 — `<OutputContainer hideHeader />` → header suppressed, so the reroll button is not rendered.
   - SettlementDetail.jsx:849 — `<OutputContainer settlement readOnly saveId />` → readOnly nulls onRegenerate, so the `&& onRegenerate` gate fails.
   - PublicDossierView.jsx:118 — `<OutputContainer settlement readOnly playerView publicChronicle />` → readOnly nulls onRegenerate.
   No live path satisfies `!hideHeader && !readOnly`, so the header reroll button (the only surface that invokes onRegenerate('history')) never renders. No test renders the component either (the one test file only imports collectChronicle and reads the source as text).

4. NPCs has a redundant in-tab reroll: renderTab `case 'npcs'` passes `onRerollNPCs: onRegenerate ? () => onRegenerate('npcs') : null` to NPCsTab (line 424). `case 'history'` (line 425) passes only `{ settlement, narrativeNote, recentEvents }` — no reroll prop — and HistoryTab.jsx has zero reroll/regen affordance (grep for reroll/regen in HistoryTab.jsx returns nothing). So NPC reroll still works via the tab even when the header is gone; history reroll does not.

5. The underlying store capability is real and fully implemented: regenSection('history') calls eng.regenHistoryPipeline and sets settlement.history (settlementSlice.js:692-694). So this is genuinely dead UI for a working feature, not a harmless no-op.

I could not refute the claim. The evidence holds end-to-end.

On severity: this is an unreachable affordance for an implemented capability — a real broken seam (a feature the user can never trigger), but it produces no wrong simulation output, no determinism break, no data/money/security impact, and no crash. That puts it at the boundary of low/medium. I keep medium because it is a genuinely dead feature path (the store deliberately implements regenHistoryPipeline and the REROLLABLE map advertises 'Reroll History'), but a reviewer could reasonably argue low.
verdicts.fix_note: Both proposed fixes are valid. The cleaner, parity-matching option is the first: in renderTab `case 'history'` (OutputContainer.jsx:425), pass a reroll prop mirroring NPCs — e.g. `onReroll: onRegenerate ? () => onRegenerate('history') : null` — and render that button inside HistoryTab (HistoryTab.jsx currently has no reroll affordance). This keeps the reroll reachable in the live editor (where hideHeader suppresses the header) and stays correctly hidden on the readOnly saved/public surfaces (where onRegenerate is null), consistent with how NPCs already behaves. Note: NPC reroll is unaffected by this bug precisely because it has the in-tab path; history lacks it.

verdicts.reasoning:
Confirmed by reading the code and enumerating every call site. The 'Reroll History' button is rendered ONLY at src/components/OutputContainer.jsx:603, inside the header block that begins at line 580, gated by `REROLLABLE[selectedTab] && onRegenerate`. For it to fire on the 'history' tab, three conditions must hold simultaneously: (1) the header block renders (requires `!hideHeader`), (2) `selectedTab === 'history'`, and (3) `onRegenerate` is non-null (requires `!readOnly`, per line 188 `const onRegenerate = readOnly ? null : storeRegenerate`).

There are exactly three live JSX mount sites for OutputContainer (verified via grep; no other createElement/JSX usages, and the three test files do not mount the header path):
- GenerateWizard.jsx:917 `<OutputContainer hideHeader />` — the live editor. Not readOnly (so onRegenerate IS non-null), but `hideHeader` makes the header block at line 580 return null, so the button is never rendered.
- SettlementDetail.jsx:849 `<OutputContainer ... readOnly saveId={saveId} />` — saved view. readOnly nulls onRegenerate (line 188), so condition (3) fails; button never renders.
- PublicDossierView.jsx:118 `<OutputContainer ... readOnly .../>` — gallery. Same readOnly null-out.

So no live path ever satisfies all three conditions for 'history'. NPCs survives only because renderTab wires `onRerollNPCs: onRegenerate ? () => onRegenerate('npcs') : null` into NPCsTab (OutputContainer.jsx:424), and NPCsTab.jsx:68 renders its own in-tab Reroll button — that path IS reachable in the live editor. The `case 'history'` at OutputContainer.jsx:425 passes no reroll prop, and HistoryTab (src/components/new/tabs/HistoryTab.jsx:25 — props `{settlement, narrativeNote, recentEvents}`) has no reroll affordance and no RefreshCw/reroll references.

Meanwhile the underlying capability is fully implemented and working: store/settlementSlice.js:692-694 handles `regenSection('history')` by calling `eng.regenHistoryPipeline` and assigning the new history; aiSlice.js:157 even has a 'Retelling the past' progress label. So a real, functioning store capability has zero reachable UI on any live path — a dead-UI / unfinished seam, exactly as claimed.

Severity is low, not high: this does not lose data, spend/refund money, break auth/RLS, crash a main path, or corrupt simulation output/determinism. It is a missing/unreachable feature surface — the DM simply cannot reroll the history section anywhere in the shipped product. The trace is concrete and the consequence is purely 'feature unavailable', hence low.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested framing is accurate. Preferred fix mirrors the working NPCs pattern rather than deleting the capability: (1) in OutputContainer.jsx:425 change `case 'history'` to pass `onRerollHistory: onRegenerate ? () => onRegenerate('history') : null` to HistoryTab; (2) add an in-tab reroll button in HistoryTab.jsx (matching NPCsTab.jsx:68's `{onRerollNPCs && <button .../>}` pattern, gated on the new prop). This keeps the affordance alive on the live editor path (GenerateWizard, which is !readOnly) where it can actually function, and degrades gracefully to null on readOnly/public surfaces. The `history` entry in REROLLABLE (line 110) and the header button (line 603) are dead on every current path because the header is either suppressed (hideHeader) or onRegenerate is nulled (readOnly); they could be left as-is (harmless) or removed, but the actual user-facing fix must add the in-tab button as above. Simply deleting `history` from REROLLABLE would NOT restore the feature (it would just remove already-dead config) and would orphan the working regenHistoryPipeline path.

confirmed.title: Advanced wizard 'Ready to Generate' state renders a ghost 'Step 5: General Configuration' banner with an empty step card and duplicate nav
confirmed.evidence: Clicking 'Ready to Generate' calls `setWizardStep(STEPS.length)` (=4, line 765); configSlice.setWizardStep stores it unclamped (configSlice.js:65-66). The step block at line 693 is gated only on `isAdvanced && !settlement` — not on `wizardStep < STEPS.length` — so at step 4 it still renders: the hint banner shows 'Step {wizardStep+1}: {currentStepDef.label}' = 'Step 5: General Configuration' (line 704, since `STEPS[4] || STEPS[0]` falls back at line 591), an aria-label 'Step 5 of 4' (line 718), a step card whose body is empty (none of the `wizardStep === 0..3` branches match, lines 727-730) but whose header still says 'General Configuration', plus a second 'Ready to Generate' button (line 764) — all stacked above the intended WizardCloseout (line 783) and Generate button.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Gate the step-indicator/content/navigation block on `wizardStep < STEPS.length` (e.g. `isAdvanced && !settlement && wizardStep < STEPS.length`), leaving WizardCloseout + Generate as the sole final-state surface.

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified against the actual code. STEPS has exactly 4 entries (GenerateWizard.jsx:77-98), so STEPS.length === 4. The final-step "Ready to Generate" button calls setWizardStep(STEPS.length) = setWizardStep(4) (line 765). configSlice.setWizardStep stores the value unclamped (configSlice.js:65-66), and there is no clamping anywhere else: store/index.js onRehydrateStorage only resets wizardStep to 0 (line 79), and wizardStep is not persisted. So wizardStep === 4 is a genuine reachable render state on the advanced (signed-in) generate path.

At wizardStep === 4 the render produces exactly what the reviewer describes:
- Line 591: currentStepDef = STEPS[4] || STEPS[0] → STEPS[4] is undefined, falls back to STEPS[0] = { label: 'General Configuration' }.
- Line 693: the step-indicator/content/nav block is gated ONLY on `isAdvanced && !settlement` (both still true), so it still renders.
- Line 704: hint banner shows "Step 5: General Configuration" (wizardStep+1 = 5).
- Line 718: aria-label "Step 5 of 4: General Configuration" — a real, incorrect screen-reader announcement.
- Lines 727-730: none of wizardStep === 0..3 match, so the card body is empty, yet its header (lines 722-724) still reads "General Configuration".
- Line 751: `wizardStep < STEPS.length - 1` → 4 < 3 is false, so the else branch (line 764) renders a SECOND "Ready to Generate" button.
- Simultaneously, line 783 (`wizardStep >= STEPS.length` → 4 >= 4 true) renders WizardCloseout, and line 789 renders the Generate button. WizardCloseout.jsx has no flag gate and no early null return, so the comment at GenerateWizard.jsx:782 claiming it "self-gates on the flag" is stale — it always renders.

Net result: the intended final surface (WizardCloseout + Generate) is stacked beneath a leftover ghost "Step 5 of 4: General Configuration" banner, an empty step card, and a duplicate "Ready to Generate" button. Refutation attempts all failed: there is no upstream clamp, no flag turning the block off, the path is reachable for any signed-in advanced-mode user, and no test covers the wizardStep === 4 state (generateWizardFocus.test.jsx only exercises steps 0/1; wizardCloseout.test.jsx tests the component in isolation). Evidence holds.

Severity is medium rather than higher because it is a visual/UX + accessibility defect, not data loss, billing, security, determinism, or a crash — the Generate button still functions. But it is on the main advanced-generate path, presents duplicate interactive controls, and emits an incorrect "Step 5 of 4" aria-label, which justifies medium over low.
verdicts.fix_note: Suggested fix is correct: gate the step-indicator/content/navigation block (line 693) on `isAdvanced && !settlement && wizardStep < STEPS.length`, leaving WizardCloseout + Generate as the sole final-state surface. Equivalently the codebase could clamp setWizardStep, but using a distinct sentinel value (STEPS.length) to mean "ready" is fine — the only fix needed is tightening the line-693 guard. Note the stale comment at line 782 ("Self-gates on the flag") should also be corrected since WizardCloseout has no flag gate.

verdicts.reasoning:
Confirmed by reading the real code and reproducing the arithmetic against the actual STEPS array.

Concrete failure path (live session, no persistence needed):
1. User picks "Advanced Generate" -> wizardMode='advanced', wizardStep=0 (setWizardMode resets step, configSlice.js:76).
2. User clicks Next three times to reach wizardStep=3 (the last step, 'Trade Dynamics').
3. At step 3 the nav else-branch renders the "Ready to Generate" button (GenerateWizard.jsx:763-774). Clicking it fires setWizardStep(STEPS.length) = setWizardStep(4) (line 765).
4. configSlice.setWizardStep stores the value UNCLAMPED: `state.wizardStep = step` (configSlice.js:65-66). There is no other setWizardStep caller and no clamp anywhere (verified by grep). wizardStep is NOT persisted (store/index.js:79 resets to 0 on rehydrate), so this is purely an in-session sentinel state.

What goes wrong at wizardStep===4:
- The "Ready to Generate" sentinel state is DELIBERATE: WizardCloseout (line 783, gated `isAdvanced && wizardStep >= STEPS.length && !settlement`) and the big Generate button (line 789, gated `settlement || (isAdvanced && wizardStep >= STEPS.length)`) are intended to render here. WizardCloseout.jsx even renders its own "Ready to generate" header + config-summary chips (lines 125-162). That part is correct.
- BUG: the per-step block at line 693 is gated ONLY on `isAdvanced && !settlement` — it omits the `wizardStep < STEPS.length` condition the closeout/generate blocks use. So at wizardStep=4 it ALSO renders, on top of the intended closeout.
- Inside that ghost block: `currentStepDef = STEPS[wizardStep] || STEPS[0]` (line 591) falls back to STEPS[0]='General Configuration'. Verified via node: the hint banner shows "Step 5: General Configuration" (line 704), the aria-label is "Step 5 of 4: General Configuration" (line 718, an impossible "5 of 4"), the step card header reads "General Configuration" (line 723) but its body is EMPTY because none of the `wizardStep === 0..3` branches match (lines 727-730), and the nav renders a SECOND "Ready to Generate" button (line 764, since `wizardStep < STEPS.length - 1` is false). StepIndicator (line 695) renders with currentStep=4, so no dot is highlighted (i===currentStep never true for i in 0..3) and all four dots show as "past".

User-visible consequence: the final pre-generation screen stacks a phantom "Step 5: General Configuration" hint banner, an empty-bodied step card mislabeled "General Configuration", a duplicate/dead "Ready to Generate" button, and a broken "Step 5 of 4" screen-reader announcement ABOVE the intended WizardCloseout summary and Generate button. Confusing, contradictory UI on the commit step of the advanced flow; not a crash, no data loss, no determinism/billing impact — hence medium. No test covers this state: generateWizardFocus.test.jsx only exercises wizardStep 0 and 1 (lines 65, 81).

I traced/reproduced the index math with node against the literal STEPS array and confirmed every gating boolean. Confidence high (~0.9): a reviewer with the file open will see the line-693 gate diverges from the line-783/789 gates that share the same sentinel.
verdicts.fix_note:
Suggested fix (add a step-range guard to the line-693 block) is correct and matches the convention already used by the closeout (line 783) and generate (line 789) blocks. Concretely, change the gate at line 693 from `{isAdvanced && !settlement && (` to `{isAdvanced && !settlement && wizardStep < STEPS.length && (`. That cleanly suppresses the entire StepIndicator+hint+step-card+nav fragment in the sentinel state while leaving steps 0..3 untouched, restoring a single "Ready to Generate" surface (WizardCloseout + Generate button).

Two secondary notes, not blockers: (1) The `STEPS[wizardStep] || STEPS[0]` fallback at line 591 is what turns this into plausible-but-wrong content rather than a crash on `currentStepDef.label` — leave it, but it's the reason the bug renders silently. (2) A more defensive belt-and-suspenders option is to also clamp in configSlice.setWizardStep, but since wizardStep===STEPS.length is a *legitimate* sentinel the closeout/generate blocks rely on, do NOT clamp it to STEPS.length-1 there or you'd break the Ready-to-Generate state entirely; the right fix is purely the view-layer guard at line 693.

confirmed.title: Gallery detail ignores browser Back: navigating /gallery/:slug → /gallery leaves the dossier open while the URL shows the list
confirmed.evidence: GalleryPage renders the detail view whenever `activeSlug` is truthy (GalleryPage.jsx:44). `activeSlug` is set by openDossier and cleared only by the in-app backToList button (line 143-150). The route-sync effect (lines 115-124) handles routeSlug becoming truthy (opens the dossier) but when routeSlug transitions slug → null (browser Back from /gallery/:slug to /gallery), it falls through to the legacy `?slug=` check and does nothing — `activeSlug`/`dossier` are never cleared. Result: after pressing Back, the URL is /gallery but the detail view stays mounted; Back appears broken and the list is unreachable except via the in-app button.
confirmed.suggested_fix: In the routeSlug effect, when `routeSlug` is null (and no legacy ?slug param), clear the detail state: setActiveSlug(null); setDossier(null); setDossierError(null).

verdicts.reasoning:
Verified the full chain end-to-end and could not refute it.

ROUTE LAYER (src/lib/routes.js): `/gallery/:slug` matches the param route (line 58) → `params.slug = slug`. Bare `/gallery` hits the exact-match branch (line 131) → `params: {}`, so `params.slug` is undefined. App.jsx:523 passes `routeSlug={params.slug}` with no `key`, so the slug → undefined transition flows into the already-mounted hook without a remount.

REACTIVITY (src/hooks/useRoute.js): `useRoute()` uses `useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, currentHref)` (line 100-103), and `subscribe` listens to `popstate` (line 35). A browser Back from /gallery/:slug to /gallery fires popstate → re-resolve → GalleryPage re-renders with routeSlug=undefined. Confirmed reactive.

HOOK (src/hooks/useGalleryPageState.js): The route-sync effect (lines 115-124) depends on routeSlug (line 124). On Back: routeSlug is undefined → `if (routeSlug)` skipped; window defined → `params.get('slug')` on /gallery (no query) is null → nothing happens. The effect has no branch to clear state. `activeSlug` is set by openDossier (line 97) and cleared ONLY by backToList (line 144). GalleryPage.jsx:44 renders GalleryDetail whenever activeSlug is truthy.

NET: After browser Back, URL = /gallery but activeSlug/dossier remain set, so the detail view stays mounted while the URL shows the list — a genuine URL/UI desync and a broken browser-Back. No upstream guard clears it, the component is not keyed so it does not remount/reset, and no nearby comment marks this as intentional. The existing tests (tests/components/galleryPage.test.jsx) mock useRoute entirely and never exercise a popstate/Back transition, so nothing covers or refutes this. The cited APIs work as described.

Severity medium is honest: no data loss/crash/security/money; the in-app "Back to gallery" button (backToList) still works so the list isn't permanently unreachable, but the browser Back button is functionally broken on this feature and the URL contradicts the rendered view.
verdicts.fix_note: The suggested fix is correct and complete: in the routeSlug effect, add an explicit clear when routeSlug is falsy and no legacy ?slug param is present — setActiveSlug(null); setDossier(null); setDossierError(null) (and for full parity with backToList, also clear actionError/actionNotice). Note routeSlug arrives as undefined (not null) on the /gallery exact route, so guard on falsiness rather than strict === null. One caveat to avoid a regression: the legacy ?slug branch must still take precedence, so structure it as: if (routeSlug) {open} else if (legacy ?slug) {open} else {clear} — clearing only in the final else so a legacy ?slug deep-link is not wiped.

verdicts.reasoning:
Confirmed real and reproducible by reading the full path and simulating the deterministic control flow.

Routing: both /gallery and /gallery/:slug resolve to view:'gallery' (src/lib/routes.js resolveLocation — exact match for /gallery returns params:{}, PARAM_ROUTES match for /gallery/:slug returns {slug}). App.jsx:523 renders GalleryPage gated only on `view === 'gallery'` with no slug-derived `key`, so navigating /gallery/:slug -> /gallery keeps the component MOUNTED; only the prop routeSlug (=params.slug) changes from a slug to undefined. The useState(routeSlug || null) initializer (useGalleryPageState.js:44) runs only on mount, so it does not re-clear state.

The defect: GalleryPage.jsx:44 renders GalleryDetail whenever `activeSlug` is truthy. activeSlug is set truthy by openDossier (line 97) and, after mount, reset to null ONLY by backToList (line 144), which is wired exclusively to GalleryDetail's in-app onBack button. The route-sync effect (lines 115-124) has routeSlug as a dependency, so on browser Back it re-runs with a falsy routeSlug, skips the `if (routeSlug)` branch, falls through to the legacy `?slug=` query check (absent on a /gallery URL), and returns without touching activeSlug/dossier. Net result: URL becomes /gallery but the dossier stays mounted; the URL and the screen disagree, browser Back appears broken, and the list is reachable only via the in-app button.

Evidence trace (read-only): I confirmed view stays 'gallery' across the transition (routes.js), confirmed App passes routeSlug={params.slug} with no remount key (App.jsx:523), and ran a pure simulation of the exact effect/openDossier/backToList logic: landing on /gallery/:slug -> render DETAIL; browser Back to /gallery (routeSlug null, no ?slug=) -> state unchanged, still render DETAIL; in-app backToList -> render LIST. The existing tests (tests/components/galleryPage.test.jsx) never exercise the routeSlug slug->null transition, so nothing guards this either way. (Could not run vitest: node_modules is broken — missing @rollup/rollup-darwin-arm64 — and the vitest binary is sandbox-blocked; the pure-logic sim is faithful to the read source.)

Severity medium: a real, deterministic navigation/UX defect on the non-critical community-gallery browse path. No data loss, money, security, simulation-output, or determinism impact, and the in-app Back button still works so the user is not hard-stuck — but browser Back/Forward genuinely break and the URL/screen diverge.
verdicts.fix_note:
The suggested direction (handle the routeSlug slug->null transition) is correct. Add an else path in the effect (useGalleryPageState.js:115-124): when routeSlug is falsy AND there is no legacy `?slug=` param, clear the detail state — setActiveSlug(null); setDossier(null); setDossierError(null). Equivalently, calling backToList()'s reset there works. 

Two caveats for whoever implements it: (1) Do not unconditionally clear on every falsy-routeSlug run, or you risk clobbering a dossier opened via the in-app card click. openDossier sets activeSlug synchronously and then navigate('gallery', { params:{ slug } }) pushes the URL; on that same render the App re-derives params.slug to the slug, so routeSlug should be truthy when the effect re-runs for an in-app open — but keep the clear gated on (no routeSlug AND no legacy ?slug=) to be safe. (2) A more robust structural fix is to stop maintaining a separate activeSlug that can drift from the URL: derive the open/closed decision (and the active slug) from routeSlug/the legacy param directly so the URL is the single source of truth, eliminating this drift class entirely rather than patching one transition.

confirmed.title: useReaderAudience worldbuilder signals are permanently false: reads save.neighbourLinks and save.locks, fields no save ever has
confirmed.evidence: `hasUsedNeighbours` checks `Array.isArray(x.neighbourLinks) && x.neighbourLinks.length > 0` (lines 101-104) and `hasUsedLocks` checks `Array.isArray(x.locks)` (lines 105-108). Grep shows `neighbourLinks` is never written anywhere in the codebase — neighbour data lives at `save.settlement.neighbourNetwork` (every other consumer falls back to it, e.g. domain/region/graph.js:261-266, deriveSystemState.js:226). Locks are persisted at `save.campaignState.locks` and are an OBJECT, not an array (lib/saves.js:100, campaignSlice.js:185), so even at the right path Array.isArray would fail. Both behavior signals are therefore always false and 'worldbuilder' is only reachable via exportCount >= 3 (line 61) — audience-aware copy (useCopy) misclassifies exactly the multi-settlement campaign users it was built for.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Read `x.settlement?.neighbourNetwork?.length > 0` for neighbours and `Object.keys(x.campaignState?.locks || {}).length > 0` for locks (and keep computeReaderAudience's signal extraction in one tested helper).

verdicts.reasoning:
Confirmed real after reading the file, its callers, and the save data shape.

PATH for neighbours is wrong. useReaderAudience.js:101-104 reads `x.neighbourLinks` directly on each entry of `savedSettlements`. But save entries are envelopes: the settlement lives under `.settlement` (settlementSlice.js:1146 `settlement: cloneJson(s.settlement)`; persistActiveSaveLifecycle builds `{ settlement, campaignState, timestamp }`), confirmed by settlementFromSave (deriveRegionalState.js:16-19 `return input.settlement || input`). Neighbour data lives at `settlement.neighbourNetwork` (legacy `neighbourLinks` only at the settlement level). Every correct consumer goes through settlementFromSave then reads `.neighbourNetwork || .neighbourLinks` (graph.js:261-267; deriveSystemState.js:226). The envelope has no top-level `neighbourLinks`, so `Array.isArray(x.neighbourLinks)` is always false. (sampleDossier.json's top-level `neighbourLinks` is a bare settlement, not a save envelope, so it does not rescue this.) hasUsedNeighbours is permanently false.

PATH and TYPE for locks are both wrong. Locks are persisted at `save.campaignState.locks` (saves.js:100 default `locks: {}`; campaignSlice.js:185 `cloneJson(state.locks || {})`) and are an OBJECT keyed by section (setLock/clearLocks mutate `state.locks[key]` / reset to `{}`). The hook reads `x.locks` on the envelope (wrong path) AND applies `Array.isArray` (wrong type). hasUsedLocks is permanently false.

REACHABILITY/IMPACT: computeReaderAudience line 61 gates 'worldbuilder' on `savedCount >= 5 && (hasUsedNeighbours || hasUsedLocks || exportCount >= 3)`.
